{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社}} and {{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Mifune-jinja-Mumino-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = Minamide 505, [[Toba]], [[Taki District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|43.2|N|136|35|20.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Mifune Shrine: Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (*大神御蔭川神*)
Mumino Shrine: Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (*寒川比古命*) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (*寒川比女命*) | type = Mifune Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*), small-scale [[Shikinai-sha]]
Mumino Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 ([[804 CE]]) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] (*神明造*) | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) }} '''{{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社}}''' (*Mifune Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Taki]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. This article also covers the co-located '''{{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社}}''' (*Mumino Jinja*), a subordinate shrine (*massha*). Both shrines enshrine deities associated with rivers and water (*[[Suijin|water deities]]*).Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 64. == Overview == ここでは2社共通事項を記述する。 [[三重県]][[多気郡]][[多気町]]土羽（とば、どば）に鎮座する式内社研究会 編（1990）：378ページ。鎮座地の[[地名]]「土羽」は「止場」を意味し、[[倭姫命]]の船が止まったことに由来する金子（1983）：55ページ。 [[社殿]]は[[神明造]]で[[玉垣]]に囲まれている。[[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない。社地の入り口には[[常夜灯]]が立つ。社地の[[面積]]は975[[坪]]（≒3,223[[平方メートル|m2]]）式内社研究会 編（1990）：380ページ。 == 御船神社 == 御船神社は、内宮の摂社27社のうち、第24位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ。倭姫命が外城田川（ときだがわ）を遡上する旅の途上に船を停泊し、定めた神社である。倭姫命がここで止まったのは、川幅が狭くなり船が通れなくなったからである。 『延喜式神名帳』では、「大神乃御船神社」と記す。「大神乃御船」とは[[アマテラス|天照大神]]がお乗りになる船を意味するものと見られ、薗田守良は、天照大神の[[神体]]を奉安する御樋代を納める御船代（みふなしろ）が遷御後に御船神社に納められたのではないか、と推定した。 deityは大神御蔭川神（おおかみのみかげかわのかみ）学研パブリッシング（2013）：68ページ。田畑を潤す外城田川の[[守護神]]または水上交通の守護神とされる。御船神社と同じ内宮摂社の{{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社}}でも同じ[[神 (神道)|神]]を祀る金子（1983）：59ページ。「御蔭」には[[霊魂]]、恩恵（＝おかげ）の意味があり、[[神田]]に引水する川のほとりに祀った大神の霊であると『神宮典略』では解説している式内社研究会 編（1990）：379ページ。 deityには異説があり、『伊勢二所太神宮神名秘書』は「大神乃御船神」、『延喜式神名帳僻案集』は「天鳥船神」、『伊勢国神名帳考證』では「鳥石楠船神」とする。 == 牟弥乃神社 == 牟弥乃神社は、内宮の末社16社のうち、第15位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ。社殿は中絶し、御船神社と同座する宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：14ページ。 deityは寒川比古命（さむかわひこのみこと）と寒川比女命（さむかわひめのみこと）。外城田川の守護神で、大水上命（おおみなかみのみこと）の子である。deity名の「寒川」は外城田川の異名であり、川の水が冷たく、寒かったことから倭姫命が命名した。 == History == 伊勢神宮の摂社の定義より御船神社は『延喜式神名帳』成立、すなわち[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927]]）以前に創建された伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ。『皇大神宮儀式帳』には「倭姫内親王定祝」とある。『皇大神宮儀式帳』に記載があることから[[延暦]]23年（[[804]]）以前から存在したことになる。 [[古代]]の土羽は[[度会郡]]田辺郷に属していたが、[[大化]]5年（[[649]]）に度会郡から多気郡が分立し、同郡の有尓郷（有爾郷）に属することになった。こうした経緯もあり、『延喜式神名帳』では度会郡の項に「大神乃御船神社」として記載されている。 [[中世]]以降祭祀が断絶し、[[寛文]]3年[[8月29日 (旧暦)|8月29日]]（[[Gregorian Calendar]]：[[1663]][[9月30日]]）に現社地で再興された。この時、[[堀]]の跡や古い柱の穴や朽ちた木が発見されたため、旧社地と考えられた式内社研究会 編（1990）：378 - 379ページ。[[御巫清直]]はこの地を旧社地に比定する説を支持したが、中川経雅は土羽村の隣の村にある、里人が「御船社」と俗称する[[氏神]]が旧社地であると主張した。[[天保]]14年（[[1843]]）、神社の入り口に常夜灯が設置された。 [[明治4]]（[[1871]]）に中世以降社地不明となっていた牟弥乃神社を御船神社に同座させた。 == 交通 == ;公共交通 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]][[外城田駅]]より徒歩約17分（約900m）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：63ページ。外城田駅表側（南側）から[[宮川用水]]に沿って歩き、右手に見える高台が御船神社・牟弥乃神社である。 ;自家用車 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[玉城インターチェンジ|玉城IC]]より約14分（約5.9[[キロメートル|km]]）。付近に[[駐車場]]はない。 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58年8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_06.jpg 御船神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0701.html 御船神社（みふねじんじゃ） 御同座 牟弥乃神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|みふねしんしや}} [[Category:11 deprecated open]] [[Category:御船神社 (多気町)]][[ja:御船神社 (多気町)]][[Category:御船神社 (多気町)]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:多気町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:多気町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:御船神社 (多気町)]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:remove new pages from these]][[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]] [[Category:御船神社 (多気町)]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:remove new pages from these]][[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]] [[Category:11 deprecated open]] [[Category: pages]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ifune Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:ifune Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:11]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社}} and {{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Mifune-jinja-Mumino-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = Minamide 505, [[Toba]], [[Taki District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|43.2|N|136|35|20.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Mifune Shrine: Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (*大神御蔭川神*)
Mumino Shrine: Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (*寒川比古命*) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (*寒川比女命*) | type = Mifune Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*), small-scale [[Shikinai-sha]]
Mumino Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 ([[804 CE]]) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] (*神明造*) | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) }} '''{{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社}}''' (*Mifune Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Taki]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. This article also covers the co-located '''{{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社}}''' (*Mumino Jinja*), a subordinate shrine (*massha*). Both shrines enshrine deities associated with rivers and water (*[[Suijin|water deities]]*).Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 64. == Overview == ここでは2社共通事項を記述する。 [[三重県]][[多気郡]][[多気町]]土羽（とば、どば）に鎮座する式内社研究会 編（1990）：378ページ。鎮座地の[[地名]]「土羽」は「止場」を意味し、[[倭姫命]]の船が止まったことに由来する金子（1983）：55ページ。 [[社殿]]は[[神明造]]で[[玉垣]]に囲まれている。[[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない。社地の入り口には[[常夜灯]]が立つ。社地の[[面積]]は975[[坪]]（≒3,223[[平方メートル|m2]]）式内社研究会 編（1990）：380ページ。 == 御船神社 == 御船神社は、内宮の摂社27社のうち、第24位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ。倭姫命が外城田川（ときだがわ）を遡上する旅の途上に船を停泊し、定めた神社である。倭姫命がここで止まったのは、川幅が狭くなり船が通れなくなったからである。 『延喜式神名帳』では、「大神乃御船神社」と記す。「大神乃御船」とは[[アマテラス|天照大神]]がお乗りになる船を意味するものと見られ、薗田守良は、天照大神の[[神体]]を奉安する御樋代を納める御船代（みふなしろ）が遷御後に御船神社に納められたのではないか、と推定した。 deityは大神御蔭川神（おおかみのみかげかわのかみ）学研パブリッシング（2013）：68ページ。田畑を潤す外城田川の[[守護神]]または水上交通の守護神とされる。御船神社と同じ内宮摂社の{{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社}}でも同じ[[神 (神道)|神]]を祀る金子（1983）：59ページ。「御蔭」には[[霊魂]]、恩恵（＝おかげ）の意味があり、[[神田]]に引水する川のほとりに祀った大神の霊であると『神宮典略』では解説している式内社研究会 編（1990）：379ページ。 deityには異説があり、『伊勢二所太神宮神名秘書』は「大神乃御船神」、『延喜式神名帳僻案集』は「天鳥船神」、『伊勢国神名帳考證』では「鳥石楠船神」とする。 == 牟弥乃神社 == 牟弥乃神社は、内宮の末社16社のうち、第15位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ。社殿は中絶し、御船神社と同座する宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：14ページ。 deityは寒川比古命（さむかわひこのみこと）と寒川比女命（さむかわひめのみこと）。外城田川の守護神で、大水上命（おおみなかみのみこと）の子である。deity名の「寒川」は外城田川の異名であり、川の水が冷たく、寒かったことから倭姫命が命名した。 == History == 伊勢神宮の摂社の定義より御船神社は『延喜式神名帳』成立、すなわち[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927]]）以前に創建された伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ。『皇大神宮儀式帳』には「倭姫内親王定祝」とある。『皇大神宮儀式帳』に記載があることから[[延暦]]23年（[[804]]）以前から存在したことになる。 [[古代]]の土羽は[[度会郡]]田辺郷に属していたが、[[大化]]5年（[[649]]）に度会郡から多気郡が分立し、同郡の有尓郷（有爾郷）に属することになった。こうした経緯もあり、『延喜式神名帳』では度会郡の項に「大神乃御船神社」として記載されている。 [[中世]]以降祭祀が断絶し、[[寛文]]3年[[8月29日 (旧暦)|8月29日]]（[[Gregorian Calendar]]：[[1663]][[9月30日]]）に現社地で再興された。この時、[[堀]]の跡や古い柱の穴や朽ちた木が発見されたため、旧社地と考えられた式内社研究会 編（1990）：378 - 379ページ。[[御巫清直]]はこの地を旧社地に比定する説を支持したが、中川経雅は土羽村の隣の村にある、里人が「御船社」と俗称する[[氏神]]が旧社地であると主張した。[[天保]]14年（[[1843]]）、神社の入り口に常夜灯が設置された。 [[明治4]]（[[1871]]）に中世以降社地不明となっていた牟弥乃神社を御船神社に同座させた。 == 交通 == ;公共交通 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]][[外城田駅]]より徒歩約17分（約900m）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：63ページ。外城田駅表側（南側）から[[宮川用水]]に沿って歩き、右手に見える高台が御船神社・牟弥乃神社である。 ;自家用車 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[玉城インターチェンジ|玉城IC]]より約14分（約5.9[[キロメートル|km]]）。付近に[[駐車場]]はない。 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. 379ページ。[[御巫清直]]はこの地を旧社地に比定する説を支持したが、中川経雅は土羽村の隣の村にある、里人が「御船社」と俗称する[[氏神]]が旧社地であると主張した。[[天保]]14年（[[1843]]）、神社の入り口に常夜灯が設置された。 [[明治4]]（[[1871]]）に中世以降社地不明となっていた牟弥乃神社を御船神社に同座させた。 == 交通 == ;公共交通 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]][[外城田駅]]より徒歩約17分（約900m）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：63ページ。外城田駅表側（南側）から[[宮川用水]]に沿って歩き、右手に見える高台が御船神社・牟弥乃神社である。 ;自家用車 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[玉城インターチェンジ|玉城IC]]より約14分（約5.9[[キロメートル|km]]）。付近に[[駐車場]]はない。 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. {{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社}} and {{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Mifune-jinja-Mumino-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = Minamide 505, [[Toba]], [[Taki District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|43.2|N|136|35|20.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Mifune Shrine: Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (*大神御蔭川神*)
Mumino Shrine: Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (*寒川比古命*) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (*寒川比女命*) | type = Mifune Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*), small-scale [[Shikinai-sha]]
Mumino Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before [[Enryaku]] 23 ([[804 CE]]) | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] (*神明造*) | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) }} '''{{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社}}''' (*Mifune Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Taki]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. This article also covers the co-located '''{{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社}}''' (*Mumino Jinja*), a subordinate shrine (*massha*). Both shrines enshrine deities associated with rivers and water (*[[Suijin|water deities]]*).Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 64. == Overview == ここでは2社共通事項を記述する。 [[三重県]][[多気郡]][[多気町]]土羽（とば、どば）に鎮座する式内社研究会 編（1990）：378ページ。鎮座地の[[地名]]「土羽」は「止場」を意味し、[[倭姫命]]の船が止まったことに由来する金子（1983）：55ページ。 [[社殿]]は[[神明造]]で[[玉垣]]に囲まれている。[[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない。社地の入り口には[[常夜灯]]が立つ。社地の[[面積]]は975[[坪]]（≒3,223[[平方メートル|m2]]）式内社研究会 編（1990）：380ページ。 == 御船神社 == 御船神社は、内宮の摂社27社のうち、第24位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ。倭姫命が外城田川（ときだがわ）を遡上する旅の途上に船を停泊し、定めた神社である。倭姫命がここで止まったのは、川幅が狭くなり船が通れなくなったからである。 『延喜式神名帳』では、「大神乃御船神社」と記す。「大神乃御船」とは[[アマテラス|天照大神]]がお乗りになる船を意味するものと見られ、薗田守良は、天照大神の[[神体]]を奉安する御樋代を納める御船代（みふなしろ）が遷御後に御船神社に納められたのではないか、と推定した。 deityは大神御蔭川神（おおかみのみかげかわのかみ）学研パブリッシング（2013）：68ページ。田畑を潤す外城田川の[[守護神]]または水上交通の守護神とされる。御船神社と同じ内宮摂社の{{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社}}でも同じ[[神 (神道)|神]]を祀る金子（1983）：59ページ。「御蔭」には[[霊魂]]、恩恵（＝おかげ）の意味があり、[[神田]]に引水する川のほとりに祀った大神の霊であると『神宮典略』では解説している式内社研究会 編（1990）：379ページ。 deityには異説があり、『伊勢二所太神宮神名秘書』は「大神乃御船神」、『延喜式神名帳僻案集』は「天鳥船神」、『伊勢国神名帳考證』では「鳥石楠船神」とする。 == 牟弥乃神社 == 牟弥乃神社は、内宮の末社16社のうち、第15位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ。社殿は中絶し、御船神社と同座する宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：14ページ。 deityは寒川比古命（さむかわひこのみこと）と寒川比女命（さむかわひめのみこと）。外城田川の守護神で、大水上命（おおみなかみのみこと）の子である。deity名の「寒川」は外城田川の異名であり、川の水が冷たく、寒かったことから倭姫命が命名した。 == History == 伊勢神宮の摂社の定義より御船神社は『延喜式神名帳』成立、すなわち[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927]]）以前に創建された伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ。『皇大神宮儀式帳』には「倭姫内親王定祝」とある。『皇大神宮儀式帳』に記載があることから[[延暦]]23年（[[804]]）以前から存在したことになる。 [[古代]]の土羽は[[度会郡]]田辺郷に属していたが、[[大化]]5年（[[649]]）に度会郡から多気郡が分立し、同郡の有尓郷（有爾郷）に属することになった。こうした経緯もあり、『延喜式神名帳』では度会郡の項に「大神乃御船神社」として記載されている。 [[中世]]以降祭祀が断絶し、[[寛文]]3年[[8月29日 (旧暦)|8月29日]]（[[Gregorian Calendar]]：[[1663]][[9月30日]]）に現社地で再興された。この時、[[堀]]の跡や古い柱の穴や朽ちた木が発見されたため、旧社地と考えられた式内社研究会 編（1990）：378 - 379ページ。[[御巫清直]]はこの地を旧社地に比定する説を支持したが、中川経雅は土羽村の隣の村にある、里人が「御船社」と俗称する[[氏神]]が旧社地であると主張した。[[天保]]14年（[[1843]]）、神社の入り口に常夜灯が設置された。 [[明治4]]（[[1871]]）に中世以降社地不明となっていた牟弥乃神社を御船神社に同座させた。 == 交通 == ;公共交通 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]][[外城田駅]]より徒歩約17分（約900m）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：63ページ。外城田駅表側（南側）から[[宮川用水]]に沿って歩き、右手に見える高台が御船神社・牟弥乃神社である。 ;自家用車 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[玉城インターチェンジ|玉城IC]]より約14分（約5.9[[キロメートル|km]]）。付近に[[駐車場]]はない。 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. Both shrines enshrine deities associated with rivers and water (*[[Suijin|water deities]]*).Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 64. == Overview == ここでは2社共通事項を記述する。 [[三重県]][[多気郡]][[多気町]]土羽（とば、どば）に鎮座する式内社研究会 編（1990）：378ページ。鎮座地の[[地名]]「土羽」は「止場」を意味し、[[倭姫命]]の船が止まったことに由来する金子（1983）：55ページ。 [[社殿]]は[[神明造]]で[[玉垣]]に囲まれている。[[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない。社地の入り口には[[常夜灯]]が立つ。社地の[[面積]]は975[[坪]]（≒3,223[[平方メートル|m2]]）式内社研究会 編（1990）：380ページ。 == 御船神社 == 御船神社は、内宮の摂社27社のうち、第24位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ。倭姫命が外城田川（ときだがわ）を遡上する旅の途上に船を停泊し、定めた神社である。倭姫命がここで止まったのは、川幅が狭くなり船が通れなくなったからである。 『延喜式神名帳』では、「大神乃御船神社」と記す。「大神乃御船」とは[[アマテラス|天照大神]]がお乗りになる船を意味するものと見られ、薗田守良は、天照大神の[[神体]]を奉安する御樋代を納める御船代（みふなしろ）が遷御後に御船神社に納められたのではないか、と推定した。 deityは大神御蔭川神（おおかみのみかげかわのかみ）学研パブリッシング（2013）：68ページ。田畑を潤す外城田川の[[守護神]]または水上交通の守護神とされる。御船神社と同じ内宮摂社の{{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社}}でも同じ[[神 (神道)|神]]を祀る金子（1983）：59ページ。「御蔭」には[[霊魂]]、恩恵（＝おかげ）の意味があり、[[神田]]に引水する川のほとりに祀った大神の霊であると『神宮典略』では解説している式内社研究会 編（1990）：379ページ。 deityには異説があり、『伊勢二所太神宮神名秘書』は「大神乃御船神」、『延喜式神名帳僻案集』は「天鳥船神」、『伊勢国神名帳考證』では「鳥石楠船神」とする。 == 牟弥乃神社 == 牟弥乃神社は、内宮の末社16社のうち、第15位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ。社殿は中絶し、御船神社と同座する宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：14ページ。 deityは寒川比古命（さむかわひこのみこと）と寒川比女命（さむかわひめのみこと）。外城田川の守護神で、大水上命（おおみなかみのみこと）の子である。deity名の「寒川」は外城田川の異名であり、川の水が冷たく、寒かったことから倭姫命が命名した。 == History == 伊勢神宮の摂社の定義より御船神社は『延喜式神名帳』成立、すなわち[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927]]）以前に創建された伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ。『皇大神宮儀式帳』には「倭姫内親王定祝」とある。『皇大神宮儀式帳』に記載があることから[[延暦]]23年（[[804]]）以前から存在したことになる。 [[古代]]の土羽は[[度会郡]]田辺郷に属していたが、[[大化]]5年（[[649]]）に度会郡から多気郡が分立し、同郡の有尓郷（有爾郷）に属することになった。こうした経緯もあり、『延喜式神名帳』では度会郡の項に「大神乃御船神社」として記載されている。 [[中世]]以降祭祀が断絶し、[[寛文]]3年[[8月29日 (旧暦)|8月29日]]（[[Gregorian Calendar]]：[[1663]][[9月30日]]）に現社地で再興された。この時、[[堀]]の跡や古い柱の穴や朽ちた木が発見されたため、旧社地と考えられた式内社研究会 編（1990）：378 - 379ページ。[[御巫清直]]はこの地を旧社地に比定する説を支持したが、中川経雅は土羽村の隣の村にある、里人が「御船社」と俗称する[[氏神]]が旧社地であると主張した。[[天保]]14年（[[1843]]）、神社の入り口に常夜灯が設置された。 [[明治4]]（[[1871]]）に中世以降社地不明となっていた牟弥乃神社を御船神社に同座させた。 == 交通 == ;公共交通 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]][[外城田駅]]より徒歩約17分（約900m）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：63ページ。外城田駅表側（南側）から[[宮川用水]]に沿って歩き、右手に見える高台が御船神社・牟弥乃神社である。 ;自家用車 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[玉城インターチェンジ|玉城IC]]より約14分（約5.9[[キロメートル|km]]）。付近に[[駐車場]]はない。 == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. Mumino Shrine: Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (*寒川比古命*) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (*寒川比女命*) | type = Mifune Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*), small-scale {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}
Mumino Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 23 ({{ill|804 CE|ja|804}}) | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} (*神明造*) | festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (*神嘗祭*) }} '''{{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社}}''' (*Mifune Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}), located in {{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan. This article also covers the co-located '''{{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社}}''' (*Mumino Jinja*), a subordinate shrine (*massha*). Both shrines enshrine deities associated with rivers and water (*{{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}*).Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 64. == Overview == This section describes the common aspects of the two shrines. {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}{{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町}} '''Toba''' (土羽) is enshrined at the location.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378ページ The place name {{ill|place name|ja|地名}} “Toba” (土羽) means “stopping place”, and it is derived from the boat of {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} having stopped there.金子（1983）：55ページ The shrine building {{ill|Shrine building|ja|社殿}} is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style and is surrounded by a {{ill|Gyokugaki|ja|玉垣}}. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭}}. An {{ill|eternal lantern|ja|常夜灯}} stands at the entrance of the shrine grounds. The area of the shrine grounds is 975 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪}} (≈3,223 {{ill|square meter|ja|平方メートル|lt=m2}}).式内社研究会 編（1990）：380ページ == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) is the 24th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ It is the shrine established when {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}, while journeying upstream on the Tokidagawa (外城田川, Tokidagawa), anchored her boat and designated the site. She stopped here because the river narrowed and her boat could no longer pass. In the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is recorded as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine” (大神乃御船神社). The term “Ōkami no Mifune” is understood to mean the boat on which {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} rides, and Sonoda Moriyoshi conjectured that the boat-keeper’s office, which enshrined the sacred object of Amaterasu, was transferred to Mifune Shrine after her relocation. The deity enshrined is Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (大神御蔭川神).学研パブリッシング（2013）：68ページ It is regarded as either the guardian deity ({{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神}}) of the Tokidagawa, which irrigates the fields, or as the protector of water transportation. The same deity is also enshrined at {{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, as a {{ill|deity|ja|神}}.金子（1983）：59ページ The term “Mikage” denotes a spirit or beneficence, and the ''Jingū Tenryaku'' explains that it is the spirit of the great deity enshrined by the river that diverts water to {{ill|Kanda|ja|神田}}.式内社研究会 編（1990）：379ページ There are alternative theories regarding the deity; the ''Ise Nisho Taijingū Shinmyōhisho'' calls it “Ōkami no Mifune Shin”, the ''Engishiki Shinmyōchō Hetsuan-shū'' refers to it as “Tenchōsen Shin”, and the ''Ise-koku Shinmyōchō Kōshō'' names it “Toriishi Kusen Shin”. == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) is the 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ Its shrine building is no longer extant and it is co-located with Mifune Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：14ページ The deities enshrined are Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (寒川比古命) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (寒川比女命). They are regarded as the guardian deities of the Tokidagawa and are the children of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命). The name “Samukawa” is an alternate name for the Tokidagawa, derived from the fact that its water was cold, which led {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} to name it so. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, Mifune Shrine was established by the time the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'' was compiled, that is, before the 5th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長 (元号)|lt=Enchō}} ({{ill|927|ja|927}}).伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ The ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'' records it as “Wahime Naishinnō Teishuku” (倭姫内親王定祝). Since it is mentioned in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'', it is inferred that the shrine existed prior to the 23rd year of {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} ({{ill|804|ja|804}}). In ancient times, Toba belonged to the Tanabe area of {{ill|Togai District|ja|度会郡}} (田辺郷). However, in the 5th year of {{ill|Taika|ja|大化}} ({{ill|649|ja|649}}), {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}} was separated from Togai District, and Toba came to belong to the Yūri area (有尓郷) of the new district. Accordingly, in the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is listed under Togai District as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine”. From the {{ill|Medieval|ja|中世}} period onwards, the rites were discontinued, and the shrine was revived at its present location in the 3rd year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} ([[8月29日 (旧暦)|8月29日]] {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}}: {{ill|1663|ja|1663}}, {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日}}). At that time, traces of a moat ({{ill|Moat|ja|堀}}), old pillar holes, and decayed wood were discovered, suggesting the site of the original shrine.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378 - 379ページ While {{ill|Mikoto Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直}} supported the theory that this location was the original shrine site, Nakagawa Tsuneaki argued that the village deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}), colloquially known as “Mifune-sha”, of a neighboring village in Toba Village, was the original shrine. In the 14th year of {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} ({{ill|1843|ja|1843}}), an eternal lantern was installed at the shrine entrance. In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4}} ({{ill|1871|ja|1871}}), Mumino Shrine, whose original location had been unknown since the Medieval period, was relocated to be co‑located with Mifune Shrine. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}} {{ill|Tokido Station|ja|外城田駅}}, it is about a 17‑minute walk (approximately 900m).伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：63ページ From the station's main (southern) exit, walk along {{ill|Miyakawa Waterway|ja|宮川用水}}, and the elevated area visible on the right is where Mifune Shrine and Mumino Shrine are located. ; By Private Car * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} {{ill|Tamashiro Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|lt=Tamashiro IC|lt_ja=玉城IC}}, it is about a 14‑minute drive (approximately 5.9 {{ill|kilometer|ja|キロメートル|lt=km}}). There is no {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場}} nearby. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎}} 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}『伊勢神宮に行こう』 Gakken Mook 神社紀行セレクション vol.1、{{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔}} 監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』 三重県郷土資料叢書 第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58年8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』 {{ill|Kogakkan University|ja|國學院大學}} 出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai-sha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt_ja=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_06.jpg 御船神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0701.html 御船神社（みふねじんじゃ） 御同座 牟弥乃神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:11 Deprecated Open]] [[Category:Mifune Shrine (Taki Town)]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Ise]] [[Category:Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines]] [[Category:History of Taki Town]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Refreshing Imported Pages]] [[Category:Bump 1-26]] [[Category:Category Refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Remove New Pages From These]] [[Category:C]] [[Category:cgoodbye $1 Tier 4]] [[Category:Tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]] [[ja:御船神社]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社}} and {{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image = [[File:Mifune-jinja-Mumino-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = Minamide 505, {{ill|Toba|ja|鳥羽}}, {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|43.2|N|136|35|20.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Mifune Shrine: Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (*大神御蔭川神*)
Mumino Shrine: Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (*寒川比古命*) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (*寒川比女命*) | type = Mifune Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*), small-scale {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}
Mumino Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 23 ({{ill|804 CE|ja|804}}) | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} (*神明造*) | festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (*神嘗祭*) }} '''{{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社}}''' (*Mifune Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}), located in {{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan. This article also covers the co-located '''{{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社}}''' (*Mumino Jinja*), a subordinate shrine (*massha*). Both shrines enshrine deities associated with rivers and water (*{{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}*).Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 64. == Overview == This section describes the common aspects of the two shrines. {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}{{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町}} '''Toba''' (土羽) is enshrined at the location.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378ページ The place name {{ill|place name|ja|地名}} “Toba” (土羽) means “stopping place”, and it is derived from the boat of {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} having stopped there.金子（1983）：55ページ The shrine building {{ill|Shrine building|ja|社殿}} is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style and is surrounded by a {{ill|Gyokugaki|ja|玉垣}}. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭}}. An {{ill|eternal lantern|ja|常夜灯}} stands at the entrance of the shrine grounds. The area of the shrine grounds is 975 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪}} (≈3,223 {{ill|square meter|ja|平方メートル|lt=m2}}).式内社研究会 編（1990）：380ページ == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) is the 24th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ It is the shrine established when {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}, while journeying upstream on the Tokidagawa (外城田川, Tokidagawa), anchored her boat and designated the site. She stopped here because the river narrowed and her boat could no longer pass. In the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is recorded as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine” (大神乃御船神社). The term “Ōkami no Mifune” is understood to mean the boat on which {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} rides, and Sonoda Moriyoshi conjectured that the boat-keeper’s office, which enshrined the sacred object of Amaterasu, was transferred to Mifune Shrine after her relocation. The deity enshrined is Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (大神御蔭川神).学研パブリッシング（2013）：68ページ It is regarded as either the guardian deity ({{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神}}) of the Tokidagawa, which irrigates the fields, or as the protector of water transportation. The same deity is also enshrined at {{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, as a {{ill|deity|ja|神}}.金子（1983）：59ページ The term “Mikage” denotes a spirit or beneficence, and the ''Jingū Tenryaku'' explains that it is the spirit of the great deity enshrined by the river that diverts water to {{ill|Kanda|ja|神田}}.式内社研究会 編（1990）：379ページ There are alternative theories regarding the deity; the ''Ise Nisho Taijingū Shinmyōhisho'' calls it “Ōkami no Mifune Shin”, the ''Engishiki Shinmyōchō Hetsuan-shū'' refers to it as “Tenchōsen Shin”, and the ''Ise-koku Shinmyōchō Kōshō'' names it “Toriishi Kusen Shin”. == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) is the 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ Its shrine building is no longer extant and it is co-located with Mifune Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：14ページ The deities enshrined are Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (寒川比古命) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (寒川比女命). They are regarded as the guardian deities of the Tokidagawa and are the children of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命). The name “Samukawa” is an alternate name for the Tokidagawa, derived from the fact that its water was cold, which led {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} to name it so. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, Mifune Shrine was established by the time the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'' was compiled, that is, before the 5th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長 (元号)|lt=Enchō}} ({{ill|927|ja|927}}).伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ The ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'' records it as “Wahime Naishinnō Teishuku” (倭姫内親王定祝). Since it is mentioned in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'', it is inferred that the shrine existed prior to the 23rd year of {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} ({{ill|804|ja|804}}). In ancient times, Toba belonged to the Tanabe area of {{ill|Togai District|ja|度会郡}} (田辺郷). However, in the 5th year of {{ill|Taika|ja|大化}} ({{ill|649|ja|649}}), {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}} was separated from Togai District, and Toba came to belong to the Yūri area (有尓郷) of the new district. Accordingly, in the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is listed under Togai District as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine”. From the {{ill|Medieval|ja|中世}} period onwards, the rites were discontinued, and the shrine was revived at its present location in the 3rd year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} ([[8月29日 (旧暦)|8月29日]] {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}}: {{ill|1663|ja|1663}}, {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日}}). At that time, traces of a moat ({{ill|Moat|ja|堀}}), old pillar holes, and decayed wood were discovered, suggesting the site of the original shrine.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378 - 379ページ While {{ill|Mikoto Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直}} supported the theory that this location was the original shrine site, Nakagawa Tsuneaki argued that the village deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}), colloquially known as “Mifune-sha”, of a neighboring village in Toba Village, was the original shrine. In the 14th year of {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} ({{ill|1843|ja|1843}}), an eternal lantern was installed at the shrine entrance. In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4}} ({{ill|1871|ja|1871}}), Mumino Shrine, whose original location had been unknown since the Medieval period, was relocated to be co‑located with Mifune Shrine. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}} {{ill|Tokido Station|ja|外城田駅}}, it is about a 17‑minute walk (approximately 900m).伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：63ページ From the station's main (southern) exit, walk along {{ill|Miyakawa Waterway|ja|宮川用水}}, and the elevated area visible on the right is where Mifune Shrine and Mumino Shrine are located. ; By Private Car * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} {{ill|Tamashiro Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|lt=Tamashiro IC|lt_ja=玉城IC}}, it is about a 14‑minute drive (approximately 5.9 {{ill|kilometer|ja|キロメートル|lt=km}}). There is no {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場}} nearby. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎}} 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}『伊勢神宮に行こう』 Gakken Mook 神社紀行セレクション vol.1、{{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔}} 監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』 三重県郷土資料叢書 第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58年8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』 {{ill|Kogakkan University|ja|國學院大學}} 出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai-sha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt_ja=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_06.jpg 御船神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0701.html 御船神社（みふねじんじゃ） 御同座 牟弥乃神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:11 Deprecated Open]] [[Category:Mifune Shrine (Taki Town)]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Ise]] [[Category:Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines]] [[Category:History of Taki Town]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Refreshing Imported Pages]] [[Category:Bump 1-26]] [[Category:Category Refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Remove New Pages From These]] [[Category:cgoodbye $1 Tier 4]] [[Category:Tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]] [[Category:11]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}}[[Category:qq]] [[ja:御船神社]]

ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』 三重県郷土資料叢書 第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58年8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』 {{ill|Kogakkan University|ja|國學院大學}} 出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai-sha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt_ja=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_06.jpg 御船神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0701.html 御船神社（みふねじんじゃ） 御同座 牟弥乃神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:11 Deprecated Open]] [[Category:Mifune Shrine (Taki Town)]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Ise]] [[Category:Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines]] [[Category:History of Taki Town]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Refreshing Imported Pages]] [[Category:Bump 1-26]] [[Category:Category Refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Remove New Pages From These]] [[Category:cgoodbye $1 Tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]] [[Category:11]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}}[[Category:qq]] [[ja:御船神社]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社}} and {{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image = [[File:Mifune-jinja-Mumino-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = Minamide 505, {{ill|Toba|ja|鳥羽}}, {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|43.2|N|136|35|20.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Mifune Shrine: Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (*大神御蔭川神*)
Mumino Shrine: Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (*寒川比古命*) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (*寒川比女命*) | type = Mifune Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*), small-scale {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}
Mumino Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 23 ({{ill|804 CE|ja|804}}) | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} (*神明造*) | festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (*神嘗祭*) }} '''{{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社}}''' (*Mifune Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}), located in {{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan. This article also covers the co-located '''{{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社}}''' (*Mumino Jinja*), a subordinate shrine (*massha*). Both shrines enshrine deities associated with rivers and water (*{{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}*).Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 64. == Overview == This section describes the common aspects of the two shrines. {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}{{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町}} '''Toba''' (土羽) is enshrined at the location.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378ページ The place name {{ill|place name|ja|地名}} “Toba” (土羽) means “stopping place”, and it is derived from the boat of {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} having stopped there.金子（1983）：55ページ The shrine building {{ill|Shrine building|ja|社殿}} is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style and is surrounded by a {{ill|Gyokugaki|ja|玉垣}}. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭}}. An {{ill|eternal lantern|ja|常夜灯}} stands at the entrance of the shrine grounds. The area of the shrine grounds is 975 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪}} (≈3,223 {{ill|square meter|ja|平方メートル|lt=m2}}).式内社研究会 編（1990）：380ページ == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) is the 24th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ It is the shrine established when {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}, while journeying upstream on the Tokidagawa (外城田川, Tokidagawa), anchored her boat and designated the site. She stopped here because the river narrowed and her boat could no longer pass. In the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is recorded as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine” (大神乃御船神社). The term “Ōkami no Mifune” is understood to mean the boat on which {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} rides, and Sonoda Moriyoshi conjectured that the boat-keeper’s office, which enshrined the sacred object of Amaterasu, was transferred to Mifune Shrine after her relocation. The deity enshrined is Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (大神御蔭川神).学研パブリッシング（2013）：68ページ It is regarded as either the guardian deity ({{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神}}) of the Tokidagawa, which irrigates the fields, or as the protector of water transportation. The same deity is also enshrined at {{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, as a {{ill|deity|ja|神}}.金子（1983）：59ページ The term “Mikage” denotes a spirit or beneficence, and the ''Jingū Tenryaku'' explains that it is the spirit of the great deity enshrined by the river that diverts water to {{ill|Kanda|ja|神田}}.式内社研究会 編（1990）：379ページ There are alternative theories regarding the deity; the ''Ise Nisho Taijingū Shinmyōhisho'' calls it “Ōkami no Mifune Shin”, the ''Engishiki Shinmyōchō Hetsuan-shū'' refers to it as “Tenchōsen Shin”, and the ''Ise-koku Shinmyōchō Kōshō'' names it “Toriishi Kusen Shin”. == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) is the 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ Its shrine building is no longer extant and it is co-located with Mifune Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：14ページ The deities enshrined are Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (寒川比古命) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (寒川比女命). They are regarded as the guardian deities of the Tokidagawa and are the children of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命). The name “Samukawa” is an alternate name for the Tokidagawa, derived from the fact that its water was cold, which led {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} to name it so. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, Mifune Shrine was established by the time the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'' was compiled, that is, before the 5th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長 (元号)|lt=Enchō}} ({{ill|927|ja|927}}).伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ The ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'' records it as “Wahime Naishinnō Teishuku” (倭姫内親王定祝). Since it is mentioned in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'', it is inferred that the shrine existed prior to the 23rd year of {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} ({{ill|804|ja|804}}). In ancient times, Toba belonged to the Tanabe area of {{ill|Togai District|ja|度会郡}} (田辺郷). However, in the 5th year of {{ill|Taika|ja|大化}} ({{ill|649|ja|649}}), {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}} was separated from Togai District, and Toba came to belong to the Yūri area (有尓郷) of the new district. Accordingly, in the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is listed under Togai District as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine”. From the {{ill|Medieval|ja|中世}} period onwards, the rites were discontinued, and the shrine was revived at its present location in the 3rd year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} ([[8月29日 (旧暦)|8月29日]] {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}}: {{ill|1663|ja|1663}}, {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日}}). At that time, traces of a moat ({{ill|Moat|ja|堀}}), old pillar holes, and decayed wood were discovered, suggesting the site of the original shrine.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378 - 379ページ While {{ill|Mikoto Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直}} supported the theory that this location was the original shrine site, Nakagawa Tsuneaki argued that the village deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}), colloquially known as “Mifune-sha”, of a neighboring village in Toba Village, was the original shrine. In the 14th year of {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} ({{ill|1843|ja|1843}}), an eternal lantern was installed at the shrine entrance. In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4}} ({{ill|1871|ja|1871}}), Mumino Shrine, whose original location had been unknown since the Medieval period, was relocated to be co‑located with Mifune Shrine. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}} {{ill|Tokido Station|ja|外城田駅}}, it is about a 17‑minute walk (approximately 900m).伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：63ページ From the station's main (southern) exit, walk along {{ill|Miyakawa Waterway|ja|宮川用水}}, and the elevated area visible on the right is where Mifune Shrine and Mumino Shrine are located. ; By Private Car * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} {{ill|Tamashiro Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|lt=Tamashiro IC|lt_ja=玉城IC}}, it is about a 14‑minute drive (approximately 5.9 {{ill|kilometer|ja|キロメートル|lt=km}}). There is no {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場}} nearby. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎}} 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}『伊勢神宮に行こう』 Gakken Mook 神社紀行セレクション vol.1、{{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔}} 監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』 三重県郷土資料叢書 第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58年8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』 {{ill|Kogakkan University|ja|國學院大學}} 出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai-sha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt_ja=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_06.jpg 御船神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0701.html 御船神社（みふねじんじゃ） 御同座 牟弥乃神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:11 Deprecated Open]] [[Category:Mifune Shrine (Taki Town)]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Ise]] [[Category:Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines]] [[Category:History of Taki Town]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Refreshing Imported Pages]] [[Category:Bump 1-26]] [[Category:Category Refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Remove New Pages From These]] [[Category:cgoodbye $1 Tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]] [[Category:11]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}}[[Category:qq]] [[ja:御船神社]]

There is no {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場}} nearby. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎}} 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8== See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai-sha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt_ja=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_06.jpg 御船神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0701.html 御船神社（みふねじんじゃ） 御同座 牟弥乃神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:11 Deprecated Open]] [[Category:Mifune Shrine (Taki Town)]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Ise]] [[Category:Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines]] [[Category:History of Taki Town]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Refreshing Imported Pages]] [[Category:Bump 1-26]] [[Category:Category Refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Remove New Pages From These]] [[Category:cgoodbye $1 Tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]] [[Category:11]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}}[[Category:qq]] [[ja:御船神社]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社}} and {{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image = [[File:Mifune-jinja-Mumino-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = Minamide 505, {{ill|Toba|ja|鳥羽}}, {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|43.2|N|136|35|20.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Mifune Shrine: Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (*大神御蔭川神*)
Mumino Shrine: Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (*寒川比古命*) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (*寒川比女命*) | type = Mifune Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*), small-scale {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}
Mumino Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 23 ({{ill|804 CE|ja|804}}) | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} (*神明造*) | festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (*神嘗祭*) }} '''{{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社}}''' (*Mifune Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}), located in {{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan. This article also covers the co-located '''{{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社}}''' (*Mumino Jinja*), a subordinate shrine (*massha*). Both shrines enshrine deities associated with rivers and water (*{{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}*).Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 64. == Overview == This section describes the common aspects of the two shrines. {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}{{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町}} '''Toba''' (土羽) is enshrined at the location.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378ページ The place name {{ill|place name|ja|地名}} “Toba” (土羽) means “stopping place”, and it is derived from the boat of {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} having stopped there.金子（1983）：55ページ The shrine building {{ill|Shrine building|ja|社殿}} is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style and is surrounded by a {{ill|Gyokugaki|ja|玉垣}}. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭}}. An {{ill|eternal lantern|ja|常夜灯}} stands at the entrance of the shrine grounds. The area of the shrine grounds is 975 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪}} (≈3,223 {{ill|square meter|ja|平方メートル|lt=m2}}).式内社研究会 編（1990）：380ページ == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) is the 24th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ It is the shrine established when {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}, while journeying upstream on the Tokidagawa (外城田川, Tokidagawa), anchored her boat and designated the site. She stopped here because the river narrowed and her boat could no longer pass. In the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is recorded as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine” (大神乃御船神社). The term “Ōkami no Mifune” is understood to mean the boat on which {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} rides, and Sonoda Moriyoshi conjectured that the boat-keeper’s office, which enshrined the sacred object of Amaterasu, was transferred to Mifune Shrine after her relocation. The deity enshrined is Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (大神御蔭川神).学研パブリッシング（2013）：68ページ It is regarded as either the guardian deity ({{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神}}) of the Tokidagawa, which irrigates the fields, or as the protector of water transportation. The same deity is also enshrined at {{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, as a {{ill|deity|ja|神}}.金子（1983）：59ページ The term “Mikage” denotes a spirit or beneficence, and the ''Jingū Tenryaku'' explains that it is the spirit of the great deity enshrined by the river that diverts water to {{ill|Kanda|ja|神田}}.式内社研究会 編（1990）：379ページ There are alternative theories regarding the deity; the ''Ise Nisho Taijingū Shinmyōhisho'' calls it “Ōkami no Mifune Shin”, the ''Engishiki Shinmyōchō Hetsuan-shū'' refers to it as “Tenchōsen Shin”, and the ''Ise-koku Shinmyōchō Kōshō'' names it “Toriishi Kusen Shin”. == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) is the 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ Its shrine building is no longer extant and it is co-located with Mifune Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：14ページ The deities enshrined are Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (寒川比古命) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (寒川比女命). They are regarded as the guardian deities of the Tokidagawa and are the children of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命). The name “Samukawa” is an alternate name for the Tokidagawa, derived from the fact that its water was cold, which led {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} to name it so. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, Mifune Shrine was established by the time the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'' was compiled, that is, before the 5th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長 (元号)|lt=Enchō}} ({{ill|927|ja|927}}).伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ The ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'' records it as “Wahime Naishinnō Teishuku” (倭姫内親王定祝). Since it is mentioned in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'', it is inferred that the shrine existed prior to the 23rd year of {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} ({{ill|804|ja|804}}). In ancient times, Toba belonged to the Tanabe area of {{ill|Togai District|ja|度会郡}} (田辺郷). However, in the 5th year of {{ill|Taika|ja|大化}} ({{ill|649|ja|649}}), {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}} was separated from Togai District, and Toba came to belong to the Yūri area (有尓郷) of the new district. Accordingly, in the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is listed under Togai District as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine”. From the {{ill|Medieval|ja|中世}} period onwards, the rites were discontinued, and the shrine was revived at its present location in the 3rd year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} ([[8月29日 (旧暦)|8月29日]] {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}}: {{ill|1663|ja|1663}}, {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日}}). At that time, traces of a moat ({{ill|Moat|ja|堀}}), old pillar holes, and decayed wood were discovered, suggesting the site of the original shrine.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378 - 379ページ While {{ill|Mikoto Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直}} supported the theory that this location was the original shrine site, Nakagawa Tsuneaki argued that the village deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}), colloquially known as “Mifune-sha”, of a neighboring village in Toba Village, was the original shrine. In the 14th year of {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} ({{ill|1843|ja|1843}}), an eternal lantern was installed at the shrine entrance. In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4}} ({{ill|1871|ja|1871}}), Mumino Shrine, whose original location had been unknown since the Medieval period, was relocated to be co‑located with Mifune Shrine. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}} {{ill|Tokido Station|ja|外城田駅}}, it is about a 17‑minute walk (approximately 900m).伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：63ページ From the station's main (southern) exit, walk along {{ill|Miyakawa Waterway|ja|宮川用水}}, and the elevated area visible on the right is where Mifune Shrine and Mumino Shrine are located. ; By Private Car * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} {{ill|Tamashiro Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|lt=Tamashiro IC|lt_ja=玉城IC}}, it is about a 14‑minute drive (approximately 5.9 {{ill|kilometer|ja|キロメートル|lt=km}}). There is no {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場}} nearby. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎}} 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8== See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai-sha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt_ja=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_06.jpg 御船神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0701.html 御船神社（みふねじんじゃ） 御同座 牟弥乃神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:11 Deprecated Open]] [[Category:Mifune Shrine (Taki Town)]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Ise]] [[Category:Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines]] [[Category:History of Taki Town]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Refreshing Imported Pages]] [[Category:Bump 1-26]] [[Category:Category Refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Remove New Pages From These]] [[Category:cgoodbye $1 Tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]] [[Category:11]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}}[[Category:qq]] [[ja:御船神社]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社}} and {{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image = [[File:Mifune-jinja-Mumino-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = Minamide 505, {{ill|Toba|ja|鳥羽}}, {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|43.2|N|136|35|20.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Mifune Shrine: Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (*大神御蔭川神*)
Mumino Shrine: Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (*寒川比古命*) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (*寒川比女命*) | type = Mifune Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*), small-scale {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}
Mumino Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 23 ({{ill|804 CE|ja|804}}) | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} (*神明造*) | festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (*神嘗祭*) }} '''{{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社}}''' (*Mifune Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}), located in {{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan. This article also covers the co-located '''{{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社}}''' (*Mumino Jinja*), a subordinate shrine (*massha*). Both shrines enshrine deities associated with rivers and water (*{{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}*).Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 64. == Overview == This section describes the common aspects of the two shrines. {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}{{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町}} '''Toba''' (土羽) is enshrined at the location.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378ページ The place name {{ill|place name|ja|地名}} “Toba” (土羽) means “stopping place”, and it is derived from the boat of {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} having stopped there.金子（1983）：55ページ The shrine building {{ill|Shrine building|ja|社殿}} is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style and is surrounded by a {{ill|Gyokugaki|ja|玉垣}}. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭}}. An {{ill|eternal lantern|ja|常夜灯}} stands at the entrance of the shrine grounds. The area of the shrine grounds is 975 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪}} (≈3,223 {{ill|square meter|ja|平方メートル|lt=m2}}).式内社研究会 編（1990）：380ページ == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) is the 24th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ It is the shrine established when {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}, while journeying upstream on the Tokidagawa (外城田川, Tokidagawa), anchored her boat and designated the site. She stopped here because the river narrowed and her boat could no longer pass. In the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is recorded as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine” (大神乃御船神社). The term “Ōkami no Mifune” is understood to mean the boat on which {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} rides, and Sonoda Moriyoshi conjectured that the boat-keeper’s office, which enshrined the sacred object of Amaterasu, was transferred to Mifune Shrine after her relocation. The deity enshrined is Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (大神御蔭川神).学研パブリッシング（2013）：68ページ It is regarded as either the guardian deity ({{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神}}) of the Tokidagawa, which irrigates the fields, or as the protector of water transportation. The same deity is also enshrined at {{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, as a {{ill|deity|ja|神}}.金子（1983）：59ページ The term “Mikage” denotes a spirit or beneficence, and the ''Jingū Tenryaku'' explains that it is the spirit of the great deity enshrined by the river that diverts water to {{ill|Kanda|ja|神田}}.式内社研究会 編（1990）：379ページ There are alternative theories regarding the deity; the ''Ise Nisho Taijingū Shinmyōhisho'' calls it “Ōkami no Mifune Shin”, the ''Engishiki Shinmyōchō Hetsuan-shū'' refers to it as “Tenchōsen Shin”, and the ''Ise-koku Shinmyōchō Kōshō'' names it “Toriishi Kusen Shin”. == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) is the 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ Its shrine building is no longer extant and it is co-located with Mifune Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：14ページ The deities enshrined are Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (寒川比古命) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (寒川比女命). They are regarded as the guardian deities of the Tokidagawa and are the children of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命). The name “Samukawa” is an alternate name for the Tokidagawa, derived from the fact that its water was cold, which led {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} to name it so. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, Mifune Shrine was established by the time the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'' was compiled, that is, before the 5th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長 (元号)|lt=Enchō}} ({{ill|927|ja|927}}).伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ The ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'' records it as “Wahime Naishinnō Teishuku” (倭姫内親王定祝). Since it is mentioned in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'', it is inferred that the shrine existed prior to the 23rd year of {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} ({{ill|804|ja|804}}). In ancient times, Toba belonged to the Tanabe area of {{ill|Togai District|ja|度会郡}} (田辺郷). However, in the 5th year of {{ill|Taika|ja|大化}} ({{ill|649|ja|649}}), {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}} was separated from Togai District, and Toba came to belong to the Yūri area (有尓郷) of the new district. Accordingly, in the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is listed under Togai District as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine”. From the {{ill|Medieval|ja|中世}} period onwards, the rites were discontinued, and the shrine was revived at its present location in the 3rd year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} ([[8月29日 (旧暦)|8月29日]] {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}}: {{ill|1663|ja|1663}}, {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日}}). At that time, traces of a moat ({{ill|Moat|ja|堀}}), old pillar holes, and decayed wood were discovered, suggesting the site of the original shrine.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378 - 379ページ While {{ill|Mikoto Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直}} supported the theory that this location was the original shrine site, Nakagawa Tsuneaki argued that the village deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}), colloquially known as “Mifune-sha”, of a neighboring village in Toba Village, was the original shrine. In the 14th year of {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} ({{ill|1843|ja|1843}}), an eternal lantern was installed at the shrine entrance. In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4}} ({{ill|1871|ja|1871}}), Mumino Shrine, whose original location had been unknown since the Medieval period, was relocated to be co‑located with Mifune Shrine. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}} {{ill|Tokido Station|ja|外城田駅}}, it is about a 17‑minute walk (approximately 900m).伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：63ページ From the station's main (southern) exit, walk along {{ill|Miyakawa Waterway|ja|宮川用水}}, and the elevated area visible on the right is where Mifune Shrine and Mumino Shrine are located. ; By Private Car * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} {{ill|Tamashiro Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|lt=Tamashiro IC|lt_ja=玉城IC}}, it is about a 14‑minute drive (approximately 5.9 {{ill|kilometer|ja|キロメートル|lt=km}}). There is no {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場}} nearby. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎}} 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. There is no {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場}} nearby. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎}} 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}『伊勢神宮に行こう』 Gakken Mook 神社紀行セレクション vol.1、{{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔}} 監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』 三重県郷土資料叢書 第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』 {{ill|Kogakkan University|ja|國學院大學}} 出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8== See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai-sha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt_ja=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_06.jpg 御船神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0701.html 御船神社（みふねじんじゃ） 御同座 牟弥乃神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:11 Deprecated Open]] [[Category:Mifune Shrine (Taki Town)]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Ise]] [[Category:Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines]] [[Category:History of Taki Town]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Refreshing Imported Pages]] [[Category:Bump 1-26]] [[Category:Category Refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Remove New Pages From These]] [[Category:cgoodbye $1 Tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]] [[Category:11]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}}[[Category:qq]] [[ja:御船神社]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社}} and {{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image = [[File:Mifune-jinja-Mumino-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = Minamide 505, {{ill|Toba|ja|鳥羽}}, {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|43.2|N|136|35|20.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Mifune Shrine: Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (*大神御蔭川神*)
Mumino Shrine: Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (*寒川比古命*) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (*寒川比女命*) | type = Mifune Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*), small-scale {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}
Mumino Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} 23 ({{ill|804 CE|ja|804}}) | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} (*神明造*) | festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (*神嘗祭*) }} '''{{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社}}''' (*Mifune Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}), located in {{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Japan. This article also covers the co-located '''{{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社}}''' (*Mumino Jinja*), a subordinate shrine (*massha*). Both shrines enshrine deities associated with rivers and water (*{{ill|water deities|ja|水神}}*).Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 64. == Overview == This section describes the common aspects of the two shrines. {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}}{{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町}} '''Toba''' (土羽) is enshrined at the location.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378ページ The place name {{ill|place name|ja|地名}} “Toba” (土羽) means “stopping place”, and it is derived from the boat of {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} having stopped there.金子（1983）：55ページ The shrine building {{ill|Shrine building|ja|社殿}} is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style and is surrounded by a {{ill|Gyokugaki|ja|玉垣}}. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭}}. An {{ill|eternal lantern|ja|常夜灯}} stands at the entrance of the shrine grounds. The area of the shrine grounds is 975 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪}} (≈3,223 {{ill|square meter|ja|平方メートル|lt=m2}}).式内社研究会 編（1990）：380ページ == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) is the 24th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ It is the shrine established when {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}, while journeying upstream on the Tokidagawa (外城田川, Tokidagawa), anchored her boat and designated the site. She stopped here because the river narrowed and her boat could no longer pass. In the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is recorded as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine” (大神乃御船神社). The term “Ōkami no Mifune” is understood to mean the boat on which {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} rides, and Sonoda Moriyoshi conjectured that the boat-keeper’s office, which enshrined the sacred object of Amaterasu, was transferred to Mifune Shrine after her relocation. The deity enshrined is Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (大神御蔭川神).学研パブリッシング（2013）：68ページ It is regarded as either the guardian deity ({{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神}}) of the Tokidagawa, which irrigates the fields, or as the protector of water transportation. The same deity is also enshrined at {{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, as a {{ill|deity|ja|神}}.金子（1983）：59ページ The term “Mikage” denotes a spirit or beneficence, and the ''Jingū Tenryaku'' explains that it is the spirit of the great deity enshrined by the river that diverts water to {{ill|Kanda|ja|神田}}.式内社研究会 編（1990）：379ページ There are alternative theories regarding the deity; the ''Ise Nisho Taijingū Shinmyōhisho'' calls it “Ōkami no Mifune Shin”, the ''Engishiki Shinmyōchō Hetsuan-shū'' refers to it as “Tenchōsen Shin”, and the ''Ise-koku Shinmyōchō Kōshō'' names it “Toriishi Kusen Shin”. == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) is the 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ Its shrine building is no longer extant and it is co-located with Mifune Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：14ページ The deities enshrined are Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (寒川比古命) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (寒川比女命). They are regarded as the guardian deities of the Tokidagawa and are the children of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命). The name “Samukawa” is an alternate name for the Tokidagawa, derived from the fact that its water was cold, which led {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} to name it so. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, Mifune Shrine was established by the time the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'' was compiled, that is, before the 5th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長 (元号)|lt=Enchō}} ({{ill|927|ja|927}}).伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ The ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'' records it as “Wahime Naishinnō Teishuku” (倭姫内親王定祝). Since it is mentioned in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'', it is inferred that the shrine existed prior to the 23rd year of {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦}} ({{ill|804|ja|804}}). In ancient times, Toba belonged to the Tanabe area of {{ill|Togai District|ja|度会郡}} (田辺郷). However, in the 5th year of {{ill|Taika|ja|大化}} ({{ill|649|ja|649}}), {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡}} was separated from Togai District, and Toba came to belong to the Yūri area (有尓郷) of the new district. Accordingly, in the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is listed under Togai District as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine”. From the {{ill|Medieval|ja|中世}} period onwards, the rites were discontinued, and the shrine was revived at its present location in the 3rd year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} ([[8月29日 (旧暦)|8月29日]] {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}}: {{ill|1663|ja|1663}}, {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日}}). At that time, traces of a moat ({{ill|Moat|ja|堀}}), old pillar holes, and decayed wood were discovered, suggesting the site of the original shrine.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378 - 379ページ While {{ill|Mikoto Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直}} supported the theory that this location was the original shrine site, Nakagawa Tsuneaki argued that the village deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}), colloquially known as “Mifune-sha”, of a neighboring village in Toba Village, was the original shrine. In the 14th year of {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} ({{ill|1843|ja|1843}}), an eternal lantern was installed at the shrine entrance. In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4}} ({{ill|1871|ja|1871}}), Mumino Shrine, whose original location had been unknown since the Medieval period, was relocated to be co‑located with Mifune Shrine. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}} {{ill|Tokido Station|ja|外城田駅}}, it is about a 17‑minute walk (approximately 900m).伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：63ページ From the station's main (southern) exit, walk along {{ill|Miyakawa Waterway|ja|宮川用水}}, and the elevated area visible on the right is where Mifune Shrine and Mumino Shrine are located. ; By Private Car * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} {{ill|Tamashiro Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|lt=Tamashiro IC|lt_ja=玉城IC}}, it is about a 14‑minute drive (approximately 5.9 {{ill|kilometer|ja|キロメートル|lt=km}}). There is no {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場}} nearby. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎}} 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}『伊勢神宮に行こう』 Gakken Mook 神社紀行セレクション vol.1、{{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔}} 監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』 三重県郷土資料叢書 第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』 {{ill|Kogakkan University|ja|國學院大學}} 出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8== See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai-sha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt_ja=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_06.jpg 御船神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0701.html 御船神社（みふねじんじゃ） 御同座 牟弥乃神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:11 Deprecated Open]] [[Category:Mifune Shrine (Taki Town)]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Ise]] [[Category:Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines]] [[Category:History of Taki Town]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Refreshing Imported Pages]] [[Category:Bump 1-26]] [[Category:Category Refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Remove New Pages From These]] [[Category:cgoodbye $1 Tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]] [[Category:11]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}}[[Category:qq]] [[ja:御船神社]]

In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4}} ({{ill|1871|ja|1871}}), Mumino Shrine, whose original location had been unknown since the Medieval period, was relocated to be co‑located with Mifune Shrine. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}} {{ill|Tokido Station|ja|外城田駅}}, it is about a 17‑minute walk (approximately 900m).伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：63ページ From the station's main (southern) exit, walk along {{ill|Miyakawa Waterway|ja|宮川用水}}, and the elevated area visible on the right is where Mifune Shrine and Mumino Shrine are located. ; By Private Car * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} {{ill|Tamashiro Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|lt=Tamashiro IC|lt_ja=玉城IC}}, it is about a 14‑minute drive (approximately 5.9 {{ill|kilometer|ja|キロメートル|lt=km}}). There is no {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場}} nearby. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎}} 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p.
