Mumino Shrine: Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (*寒川比古命*) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (*寒川比女命*) | type = Mifune Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*), small-scale {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}
Mumino Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft}} 23 ({{ill|804 CE|ja|804|12=draft}}) | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} (*神明造*) | festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} (*神嘗祭*) }} '''{{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社|12=draft}}''' (*Mifune Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}), located in {{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, Japan. This article also covers the co-located '''{{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社|12=draft}}''' (*Mumino Jinja*), a subordinate shrine (*massha*). Both shrines enshrine deities associated with rivers and water (*{{ill|water deities|ja|水神|12=draft}}*).Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 64. == Overview == This section describes the common aspects of the two shrines. {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}{{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡|12=draft}}{{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町|12=draft}} '''Toba''' (土羽) is enshrined at the location.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378ページ The place name {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=draft}} “Toba” (土羽) means “stopping place”, and it is derived from the boat of {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|12=draft}} having stopped there.金子（1983）：55ページ The shrine building {{ill|Shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft}} is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style and is surrounded by a {{ill|Gyokugaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}}. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭|12=draft}}. An {{ill|eternal lantern|ja|常夜灯|12=draft}} stands at the entrance of the shrine grounds. The area of the shrine grounds is 975 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|12=draft}} (≈3,223 {{ill|square meter|ja|平方メートル|lt=m2|12=draft}}).式内社研究会 編（1990）：380ページ == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) is the 24th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ It is the shrine established when {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|12=draft}}, while journeying upstream on the Tokidagawa (外城田川, Tokidagawa), anchored her boat and designated the site. She stopped here because the river narrowed and her boat could no longer pass. In the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is recorded as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine” (大神乃御船神社). The term “Ōkami no Mifune” is understood to mean the boat on which {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} rides, and Sonoda Moriyoshi conjectured that the boat-keeper’s office, which enshrined the sacred object of Amaterasu, was transferred to Mifune Shrine after her relocation. The deity enshrined is Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (大神御蔭川神).学研パブリッシング（2013）：68ページ It is regarded as either the guardian deity ({{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=draft}}) of the Tokidagawa, which irrigates the fields, or as the protector of water transportation. The same deity is also enshrined at {{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|12=draft}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, as a {{ill|deity|ja|神|12=draft}}.金子（1983）：59ページ The term “Mikage” denotes a spirit or beneficence, and the ''Jingū Tenryaku'' explains that it is the spirit of the great deity enshrined by the river that diverts water to {{ill|Kanda|ja|神田|12=draft}}.式内社研究会 編（1990）：379ページ There are alternative theories regarding the deity; the ''Ise Nisho Taijingū Shinmyōhisho'' calls it “Ōkami no Mifune Shin”, the ''Engishiki Shinmyōchō Hetsuan-shū'' refers to it as “Tenchōsen Shin”, and the ''Ise-koku Shinmyōchō Kōshō'' names it “Toriishi Kusen Shin”. == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) is the 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ Its shrine building is no longer extant and it is co-located with Mifune Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：14ページ The deities enshrined are Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (寒川比古命) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (寒川比女命). They are regarded as the guardian deities of the Tokidagawa and are the children of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命). The name “Samukawa” is an alternate name for the Tokidagawa, derived from the fact that its water was cold, which led {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|12=draft}} to name it so. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, Mifune Shrine was established by the time the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'' was compiled, that is, before the 5th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長 (元号)|lt=Enchō|12=draft}} ({{ill|927|ja|927|12=draft}}).伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ The ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'' records it as “Wahime Naishinnō Teishuku” (倭姫内親王定祝). Since it is mentioned in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'', it is inferred that the shrine existed prior to the 23rd year of {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft}} ({{ill|804|ja|804|12=draft}}). In ancient times, Toba belonged to the Tanabe area of {{ill|Togai District|ja|度会郡|12=draft}} (田辺郷). However, in the 5th year of {{ill|Taika|ja|大化|12=draft}} ({{ill|649|ja|649|12=draft}}), {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡|12=draft}} was separated from Togai District, and Toba came to belong to the Yūri area (有尓郷) of the new district. Accordingly, in the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is listed under Togai District as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine”. From the {{ill|Medieval|ja|中世|12=draft}} period onwards, the rites were discontinued, and the shrine was revived at its present location in the 3rd year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft}} ([[8月29日 (旧暦)|8月29日]] {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}}: {{ill|1663|ja|1663|12=draft}}, {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日|12=draft}}). At that time, traces of a moat ({{ill|Moat|ja|堀|12=draft}}), old pillar holes, and decayed wood were discovered, suggesting the site of the original shrine.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378 - 379ページ While {{ill|Mikoto Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|12=draft}} supported the theory that this location was the original shrine site, Nakagawa Tsuneaki argued that the village deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft}}), colloquially known as “Mifune-sha”, of a neighboring village in Toba Village, was the original shrine. In the 14th year of {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} ({{ill|1843|ja|1843|12=draft}}), an eternal lantern was installed at the shrine entrance. In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4|12=draft}} ({{ill|1871|ja|1871|12=draft}}), Mumino Shrine, whose original location had been unknown since the Medieval period, was relocated to be co‑located with Mifune Shrine. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} {{ill|Tokido Station|ja|外城田駅|12=draft}}, it is about a 17‑minute walk (approximately 900m).伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：63ページ From the station's main (southern) exit, walk along {{ill|Miyakawa Waterway|ja|宮川用水|12=draft}}, and the elevated area visible on the right is where Mifune Shrine and Mumino Shrine are located. ; By Private Car * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Tamashiro Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|lt=Tamashiro IC|lt_ja=玉城IC|12=draft}}, it is about a 14‑minute drive (approximately 5.9 {{ill|kilometer|ja|キロメートル|lt=km|12=draft}}). There is no {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|12=draft}} nearby. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|12=draft}} 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=draft}}『伊勢神宮に行こう』 Gakken Mook 神社紀行セレクション vol.1、{{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔|12=draft}} 監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』 三重県郷土資料叢書 第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』 {{ill|Kogakkan University|ja|國學院大學|12=draft}} 出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai-sha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt_ja=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_06.jpg 御船神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0701.html 御船神社（みふねじんじゃ） 御同座 牟弥乃神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11 Deprecated Open]] [[Category:Mifune Shrine (Taki Town)]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Ise]] [[Category:Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines]] [[Category:History of Taki Town]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Refreshing Imported Pages]] [[Category:Category Refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Remove New Pages From These]] [[Category:cgoodbye $1 Tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:御船神社]] [[sn:Mifune Shrine]]

ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai-sha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt_ja=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_06.jpg 御船神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0701.html 御船神社（みふねじんじゃ） 御同座 牟弥乃神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11 Deprecated Open]] [[Category:Mifune Shrine (Taki Town)]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Ise]] [[Category:Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines]] [[Category:History of Taki Town]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Refreshing Imported Pages]] [[Category:Category Refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Remove New Pages From These]] [[Category:cgoodbye $1 Tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:御船神社]] [[sn:Mifune Shrine]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} | image = [[File:Mifune-jinja-Mumino-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = Minamide 505, {{ill|Toba|ja|鳥羽|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|43.2|N|136|35|20.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Mifune Shrine: Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (*大神御蔭川神*)
Mumino Shrine: Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (*寒川比古命*) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (*寒川比女命*) | type = Mifune Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*), small-scale {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}
Mumino Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft}} 23 ({{ill|804 CE|ja|804|12=draft}}) | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} (*神明造*) | festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} (*神嘗祭*) }} '''{{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社|12=draft}}''' (*Mifune Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}), located in {{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, Japan. This article also covers the co-located '''{{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社|12=draft}}''' (*Mumino Jinja*), a subordinate shrine (*massha*). Both shrines enshrine deities associated with rivers and water (*{{ill|water deities|ja|水神|12=draft}}*).Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 64. == Overview == This section describes the common aspects of the two shrines. {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}{{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡|12=draft}}{{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町|12=draft}} '''Toba''' (土羽) is enshrined at the location.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378ページ The place name {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=draft}} “Toba” (土羽) means “stopping place”, and it is derived from the boat of {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|12=draft}} having stopped there.金子（1983）：55ページ The shrine building {{ill|Shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft}} is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style and is surrounded by a {{ill|Gyokugaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}}. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭|12=draft}}. An {{ill|eternal lantern|ja|常夜灯|12=draft}} stands at the entrance of the shrine grounds. The area of the shrine grounds is 975 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|12=draft}} (≈3,223 {{ill|square meter|ja|平方メートル|lt=m2|12=draft}}).式内社研究会 編（1990）：380ページ == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) is the 24th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ It is the shrine established when {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|12=draft}}, while journeying upstream on the Tokidagawa (外城田川, Tokidagawa), anchored her boat and designated the site. She stopped here because the river narrowed and her boat could no longer pass. In the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is recorded as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine” (大神乃御船神社). The term “Ōkami no Mifune” is understood to mean the boat on which {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} rides, and Sonoda Moriyoshi conjectured that the boat-keeper’s office, which enshrined the sacred object of Amaterasu, was transferred to Mifune Shrine after her relocation. The deity enshrined is Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (大神御蔭川神).学研パブリッシング（2013）：68ページ It is regarded as either the guardian deity ({{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=draft}}) of the Tokidagawa, which irrigates the fields, or as the protector of water transportation. The same deity is also enshrined at {{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|12=draft}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, as a {{ill|deity|ja|神|12=draft}}.金子（1983）：59ページ The term “Mikage” denotes a spirit or beneficence, and the ''Jingū Tenryaku'' explains that it is the spirit of the great deity enshrined by the river that diverts water to {{ill|Kanda|ja|神田|12=draft}}.式内社研究会 編（1990）：379ページ There are alternative theories regarding the deity; the ''Ise Nisho Taijingū Shinmyōhisho'' calls it “Ōkami no Mifune Shin”, the ''Engishiki Shinmyōchō Hetsuan-shū'' refers to it as “Tenchōsen Shin”, and the ''Ise-koku Shinmyōchō Kōshō'' names it “Toriishi Kusen Shin”. == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) is the 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ Its shrine building is no longer extant and it is co-located with Mifune Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：14ページ The deities enshrined are Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (寒川比古命) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (寒川比女命). They are regarded as the guardian deities of the Tokidagawa and are the children of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命). The name “Samukawa” is an alternate name for the Tokidagawa, derived from the fact that its water was cold, which led {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|12=draft}} to name it so. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, Mifune Shrine was established by the time the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'' was compiled, that is, before the 5th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長 (元号)|lt=Enchō|12=draft}} ({{ill|927|ja|927|12=draft}}).伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ The ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'' records it as “Wahime Naishinnō Teishuku” (倭姫内親王定祝). Since it is mentioned in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'', it is inferred that the shrine existed prior to the 23rd year of {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft}} ({{ill|804|ja|804|12=draft}}). In ancient times, Toba belonged to the Tanabe area of {{ill|Togai District|ja|度会郡|12=draft}} (田辺郷). However, in the 5th year of {{ill|Taika|ja|大化|12=draft}} ({{ill|649|ja|649|12=draft}}), {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡|12=draft}} was separated from Togai District, and Toba came to belong to the Yūri area (有尓郷) of the new district. Accordingly, in the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is listed under Togai District as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine”. From the {{ill|Medieval|ja|中世|12=draft}} period onwards, the rites were discontinued, and the shrine was revived at its present location in the 3rd year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft}} ([[8月29日 (旧暦)|8月29日]] {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}}: {{ill|1663|ja|1663|12=draft}}, {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日|12=draft}}). At that time, traces of a moat ({{ill|Moat|ja|堀|12=draft}}), old pillar holes, and decayed wood were discovered, suggesting the site of the original shrine.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378 - 379ページ While {{ill|Mikoto Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|12=draft}} supported the theory that this location was the original shrine site, Nakagawa Tsuneaki argued that the village deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft}}), colloquially known as “Mifune-sha”, of a neighboring village in Toba Village, was the original shrine. In the 14th year of {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} ({{ill|1843|ja|1843|12=draft}}), an eternal lantern was installed at the shrine entrance. In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4|12=draft}} ({{ill|1871|ja|1871|12=draft}}), Mumino Shrine, whose original location had been unknown since the Medieval period, was relocated to be co‑located with Mifune Shrine. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} {{ill|Tokido Station|ja|外城田駅|12=draft}}, it is about a 17‑minute walk (approximately 900m).伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：63ページ From the station's main (southern) exit, walk along {{ill|Miyakawa Waterway|ja|宮川用水|12=draft}}, and the elevated area visible on the right is where Mifune Shrine and Mumino Shrine are located. ; By Private Car * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Tamashiro Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|lt=Tamashiro IC|lt_ja=玉城IC|12=draft}}, it is about a 14‑minute drive (approximately 5.9 {{ill|kilometer|ja|キロメートル|lt=km|12=draft}}). There is no {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|12=draft}} nearby. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|12=draft}} 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=draft}}『伊勢神宮に行こう』 Gakken Mook 神社紀行セレクション vol.1、{{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔|12=draft}} 監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』 三重県郷土資料叢書 第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』 {{ill|Kogakkan University|ja|國學院大學|12=draft}} 出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai-sha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt_ja=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_06.jpg 御船神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0701.html 御船神社（みふねじんじゃ） 御同座 牟弥乃神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11 Deprecated Open]] [[Category:Mifune Shrine (Taki Town)]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Ise]] [[Category:Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines]] [[Category:History of Taki Town]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Refreshing Imported Pages]] [[Category:Category Refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Remove New Pages From These]] [[Category:cgoodbye $1 Tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:御船神社]] [[sn:Mifune Shrine]]

At that time, traces of a moat ({{ill|Moat|ja|堀|12=draft}}), old pillar holes, and decayed wood were discovered, suggesting the site of the original shrine.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378 - 379ページ While {{ill|Mikoto Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|12=draft}} supported the theory that this location was the original shrine site, Nakagawa Tsuneaki argued that the village deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft}}), colloquially known as “Mifune-sha”, of a neighboring village in Toba Village, was the original shrine. In the 14th year of {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} ({{ill|1843|ja|1843|12=draft}}), an eternal lantern was installed at the shrine entrance. In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4|12=draft}} ({{ill|1871|ja|1871|12=draft}}), Mumino Shrine, whose original location had been unknown since the Medieval period, was relocated to be co‑located with Mifune Shrine. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} {{ill|Tokido Station|ja|外城田駅|12=draft}}, it is about a 17‑minute walk (approximately 900m).伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：63ページ From the station's main (southern) exit, walk along {{ill|Miyakawa Waterway|ja|宮川用水|12=draft}}, and the elevated area visible on the right is where Mifune Shrine and Mumino Shrine are located. ; By Private Car * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Tamashiro Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|lt=Tamashiro IC|lt_ja=玉城IC|12=draft}}, it is about a 14‑minute drive (approximately 5.9 {{ill|kilometer|ja|キロメートル|lt=km|12=draft}}). There is no {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|12=draft}} nearby. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|12=draft}} 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=draft}}『伊勢神宮に行こう』 Gakken Mook 神社紀行セレクション vol.1、{{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔|12=draft}} 監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』 三重県郷土資料叢書 第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』 {{ill|Kogakkan University|ja|國學院大學|12=draft}} 出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai-sha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt_ja=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_06.jpg 御船神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0701.html 御船神社（みふねじんじゃ） 御同座 牟弥乃神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11 Deprecated Open]] [[Category:Mifune Shrine (Taki Town)]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Ise]] [[Category:Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines]] [[Category:History of Taki Town]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Refreshing Imported Pages]] [[Category:Category Refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Remove New Pages From These]] [[Category:cgoodbye $1 Tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:御船神社]] [[sn:Mifune Shrine]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|式内社|" to "|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|"
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} | image = [[File:Mifune-jinja-Mumino-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = Minamide 505, {{ill|Toba|ja|鳥羽|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|43.2|N|136|35|20.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Mifune Shrine: Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (*大神御蔭川神*)
Mumino Shrine: Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (*寒川比古命*) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (*寒川比女命*) | type = Mifune Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*), small-scale {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}}
Mumino Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft}} 23 ({{ill|804 CE|ja|804|12=draft}}) | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} (*神明造*) | festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} (*神嘗祭*) }} '''{{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社|12=draft}}''' (*Mifune Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}), located in {{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, Japan. This article also covers the co-located '''{{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社|12=draft}}''' (*Mumino Jinja*), a subordinate shrine (*massha*). Both shrines enshrine deities associated with rivers and water (*{{ill|water deities|ja|水神|12=draft}}*).Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 64. == Overview == This section describes the common aspects of the two shrines. {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}{{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡|12=draft}}{{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町|12=draft}} '''Toba''' (土羽) is enshrined at the location.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378ページ The place name {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=draft}} “Toba” (土羽) means “stopping place”, and it is derived from the boat of {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|12=draft}} having stopped there.金子（1983）：55ページ The shrine building {{ill|Shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft}} is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style and is surrounded by a {{ill|Gyokugaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}}. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭|12=draft}}. An {{ill|eternal lantern|ja|常夜灯|12=draft}} stands at the entrance of the shrine grounds. The area of the shrine grounds is 975 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|12=draft}} (≈3,223 {{ill|square meter|ja|平方メートル|lt=m2|12=draft}}).式内社研究会 編（1990）：380ページ == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) is the 24th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ It is the shrine established when {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|12=draft}}, while journeying upstream on the Tokidagawa (外城田川, Tokidagawa), anchored her boat and designated the site. She stopped here because the river narrowed and her boat could no longer pass. In the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is recorded as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine” (大神乃御船神社). The term “Ōkami no Mifune” is understood to mean the boat on which {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}} rides, and Sonoda Moriyoshi conjectured that the boat-keeper’s office, which enshrined the sacred object of Amaterasu, was transferred to Mifune Shrine after her relocation. The deity enshrined is Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (大神御蔭川神).学研パブリッシング（2013）：68ページ It is regarded as either the guardian deity ({{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=draft}}) of the Tokidagawa, which irrigates the fields, or as the protector of water transportation. The same deity is also enshrined at {{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|12=draft}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, as a {{ill|deity|ja|神|12=draft}}.金子（1983）：59ページ The term “Mikage” denotes a spirit or beneficence, and the ''Jingū Tenryaku'' explains that it is the spirit of the great deity enshrined by the river that diverts water to {{ill|Kanda|ja|神田|12=draft}}.式内社研究会 編（1990）：379ページ There are alternative theories regarding the deity; the ''Ise Nisho Taijingū Shinmyōhisho'' calls it “Ōkami no Mifune Shin”, the ''Engishiki Shinmyōchō Hetsuan-shū'' refers to it as “Tenchōsen Shin”, and the ''Ise-koku Shinmyōchō Kōshō'' names it “Toriishi Kusen Shin”. == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) is the 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ Its shrine building is no longer extant and it is co-located with Mifune Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：14ページ The deities enshrined are Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (寒川比古命) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (寒川比女命). They are regarded as the guardian deities of the Tokidagawa and are the children of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命). The name “Samukawa” is an alternate name for the Tokidagawa, derived from the fact that its water was cold, which led {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|12=draft}} to name it so. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, Mifune Shrine was established by the time the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'' was compiled, that is, before the 5th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長 (元号)|lt=Enchō|12=draft}} ({{ill|927|ja|927|12=draft}}).伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ The ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'' records it as “Wahime Naishinnō Teishuku” (倭姫内親王定祝). Since it is mentioned in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'', it is inferred that the shrine existed prior to the 23rd year of {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft}} ({{ill|804|ja|804|12=draft}}). In ancient times, Toba belonged to the Tanabe area of {{ill|Togai District|ja|度会郡|12=draft}} (田辺郷). However, in the 5th year of {{ill|Taika|ja|大化|12=draft}} ({{ill|649|ja|649|12=draft}}), {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡|12=draft}} was separated from Togai District, and Toba came to belong to the Yūri area (有尓郷) of the new district. Accordingly, in the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is listed under Togai District as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine”. From the {{ill|Medieval|ja|中世|12=draft}} period onwards, the rites were discontinued, and the shrine was revived at its present location in the 3rd year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft}} ([[8月29日 (旧暦)|8月29日]] {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}}: {{ill|1663|ja|1663|12=draft}}, {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日|12=draft}}). At that time, traces of a moat ({{ill|Moat|ja|堀|12=draft}}), old pillar holes, and decayed wood were discovered, suggesting the site of the original shrine.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378 - 379ページ While {{ill|Mikoto Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|12=draft}} supported the theory that this location was the original shrine site, Nakagawa Tsuneaki argued that the village deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft}}), colloquially known as “Mifune-sha”, of a neighboring village in Toba Village, was the original shrine. In the 14th year of {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} ({{ill|1843|ja|1843|12=draft}}), an eternal lantern was installed at the shrine entrance. In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4|12=draft}} ({{ill|1871|ja|1871|12=draft}}), Mumino Shrine, whose original location had been unknown since the Medieval period, was relocated to be co‑located with Mifune Shrine. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} {{ill|Tokido Station|ja|外城田駅|12=draft}}, it is about a 17‑minute walk (approximately 900m).伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：63ページ From the station's main (southern) exit, walk along {{ill|Miyakawa Waterway|ja|宮川用水|12=draft}}, and the elevated area visible on the right is where Mifune Shrine and Mumino Shrine are located. ; By Private Car * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Tamashiro Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|lt=Tamashiro IC|lt_ja=玉城IC|12=draft}}, it is about a 14‑minute drive (approximately 5.9 {{ill|kilometer|ja|キロメートル|lt=km|12=draft}}). There is no {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|12=draft}} nearby. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|12=draft}} 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=draft}}『伊勢神宮に行こう』 Gakken Mook 神社紀行セレクション vol.1、{{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔|12=draft}} 監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』 三重県郷土資料叢書 第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』 {{ill|Kogakkan University|ja|國學院大學|12=draft}} 出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai-sha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt_ja=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_06.jpg 御船神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0701.html 御船神社（みふねじんじゃ） 御同座 牟弥乃神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11 Deprecated Open]] [[Category:Mifune Shrine (Taki Town)]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Ise]] [[Category:Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines]] [[Category:History of Taki Town]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Refreshing Imported Pages]] [[Category:Category Refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Remove New Pages From These]] [[Category:cgoodbye $1 Tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:御船神社]] [[sn:Mifune Shrine]]

The same deity is also enshrined at {{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|12=draft}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, as a {{ill|deity|ja|神|12=draft}}.金子（1983）：59ページ The term “Mikage” denotes a spirit or beneficence, and the ''Jingū Tenryaku'' explains that it is the spirit of the great deity enshrined by the river that diverts water to {{ill|Kanda|ja|神田|12=draft}}.式内社研究会 編（1990）：379ページ There are alternative theories regarding the deity; the ''Ise Nisho Taijingū Shinmyōhisho'' calls it “Ōkami no Mifune Shin”, the ''Engishiki Shinmyōchō Hetsuan-shū'' refers to it as “Tenchōsen Shin”, and the ''Ise-koku Shinmyōchō Kōshō'' names it “Toriishi Kusen Shin”. == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) is the 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ Its shrine building is no longer extant and it is co-located with Mifune Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：14ページ The deities enshrined are Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (寒川比古命) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (寒川比女命). They are regarded as the guardian deities of the Tokidagawa and are the children of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命). The name “Samukawa” is an alternate name for the Tokidagawa, derived from the fact that its water was cold, which led {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|12=draft}} to name it so. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, Mifune Shrine was established by the time the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'' was compiled, that is, before the 5th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長 (元号)|lt=Enchō|12=draft}} ({{ill|927|ja|927|12=draft}}).伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ The ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'' records it as “Wahime Naishinnō Teishuku” (倭姫内親王定祝). Since it is mentioned in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'', it is inferred that the shrine existed prior to the 23rd year of {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft}} ({{ill|804|ja|804|12=draft}}). In ancient times, Toba belonged to the Tanabe area of {{ill|Togai District|ja|度会郡|12=draft}} (田辺郷). However, in the 5th year of {{ill|Taika|ja|大化|12=draft}} ({{ill|649|ja|649|12=draft}}), {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡|12=draft}} was separated from Togai District, and Toba came to belong to the Yūri area (有尓郷) of the new district. Accordingly, in the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is listed under Togai District as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine”. From the {{ill|Medieval|ja|中世|12=draft}} period onwards, the rites were discontinued, and the shrine was revived at its present location in the 3rd year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft}} ([[8月29日 (旧暦)|8月29日]] {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}}: {{ill|1663|ja|1663|12=draft}}, {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日|12=draft}}). At that time, traces of a moat ({{ill|Moat|ja|堀|12=draft}}), old pillar holes, and decayed wood were discovered, suggesting the site of the original shrine.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378 - 379ページ While {{ill|Mikoto Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|12=draft}} supported the theory that this location was the original shrine site, Nakagawa Tsuneaki argued that the village deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft}}), colloquially known as “Mifune-sha”, of a neighboring village in Toba Village, was the original shrine. In the 14th year of {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} ({{ill|1843|ja|1843|12=draft}}), an eternal lantern was installed at the shrine entrance. In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4|12=draft}} ({{ill|1871|ja|1871|12=draft}}), Mumino Shrine, whose original location had been unknown since the Medieval period, was relocated to be co‑located with Mifune Shrine. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} {{ill|Tokido Station|ja|外城田駅|12=draft}}, it is about a 17‑minute walk (approximately 900m).伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：63ページ From the station's main (southern) exit, walk along {{ill|Miyakawa Waterway|ja|宮川用水|12=draft}}, and the elevated area visible on the right is where Mifune Shrine and Mumino Shrine are located. ; By Private Car * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Tamashiro Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|lt=Tamashiro IC|lt_ja=玉城IC|12=draft}}, it is about a 14‑minute drive (approximately 5.9 {{ill|kilometer|ja|キロメートル|lt=km|12=draft}}). There is no {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|12=draft}} nearby. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|12=draft}} 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=draft}}『伊勢神宮に行こう』 Gakken Mook 神社紀行セレクション vol.1、{{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔|12=draft}} 監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』 三重県郷土資料叢書 第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』 {{ill|Kogakkan University|ja|國學院大學|12=draft}} 出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai-sha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt_ja=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_06.jpg 御船神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0701.html 御船神社（みふねじんじゃ） 御同座 牟弥乃神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11 Deprecated Open]] [[Category:Mifune Shrine (Taki Town)]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Ise]] [[Category:Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines]] [[Category:History of Taki Town]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Refreshing Imported Pages]] [[Category:Category Refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Remove New Pages From These]] [[Category:cgoodbye $1 Tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:御船神社]] [[sn:Mifune Shrine]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} | image = [[File:Mifune-jinja-Mumino-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = Minamide 505, {{ill|Toba|ja|鳥羽|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|43.2|N|136|35|20.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Mifune Shrine: Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (*大神御蔭川神*)
Mumino Shrine: Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (*寒川比古命*) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (*寒川比女命*) | type = Mifune Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*), small-scale {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}}
Mumino Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft}} 23 ({{ill|804 CE|ja|804|12=draft}}) | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} (*神明造*) | festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} (*神嘗祭*) }} '''{{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社|12=draft}}''' (*Mifune Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}), located in {{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, Japan. This article also covers the co-located '''{{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社|12=draft}}''' (*Mumino Jinja*), a subordinate shrine (*massha*). Both shrines enshrine deities associated with rivers and water (*{{ill|water deities|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft}}*).Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 64. == Overview == This section describes the common aspects of the two shrines. {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}{{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡|12=draft}}{{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町|12=draft}} '''Toba''' (土羽) is enshrined at the location.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378ページ The place name {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=draft}} “Toba” (土羽) means “stopping place”, and it is derived from the boat of {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|12=draft}} having stopped there.金子（1983）：55ページ The shrine building {{ill|Shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft}} is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style and is surrounded by a {{ill|Gyokugaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}}. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭|12=draft}}. An {{ill|eternal lantern|ja|常夜灯|12=draft}} stands at the entrance of the shrine grounds. The area of the shrine grounds is 975 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|12=draft}} (≈3,223 {{ill|square meter|ja|平方メートル|lt=m2|12=draft}}).式内社研究会 編（1990）：380ページ == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) is the 24th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ It is the shrine established when {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|12=draft}}, while journeying upstream on the Tokidagawa (外城田川, Tokidagawa), anchored her boat and designated the site. She stopped here because the river narrowed and her boat could no longer pass. In the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is recorded as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine” (大神乃御船神社). The term “Ōkami no Mifune” is understood to mean the boat on which {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}} rides, and Sonoda Moriyoshi conjectured that the boat-keeper’s office, which enshrined the sacred object of Amaterasu, was transferred to Mifune Shrine after her relocation. The deity enshrined is Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (大神御蔭川神).学研パブリッシング（2013）：68ページ It is regarded as either the guardian deity ({{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=draft}}) of the Tokidagawa, which irrigates the fields, or as the protector of water transportation. The same deity is also enshrined at {{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|12=draft}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, as a {{ill|deity|ja|神|12=draft}}.金子（1983）：59ページ The term “Mikage” denotes a spirit or beneficence, and the ''Jingū Tenryaku'' explains that it is the spirit of the great deity enshrined by the river that diverts water to {{ill|Kanda|ja|神田|12=draft}}.式内社研究会 編（1990）：379ページ There are alternative theories regarding the deity; the ''Ise Nisho Taijingū Shinmyōhisho'' calls it “Ōkami no Mifune Shin”, the ''Engishiki Shinmyōchō Hetsuan-shū'' refers to it as “Tenchōsen Shin”, and the ''Ise-koku Shinmyōchō Kōshō'' names it “Toriishi Kusen Shin”. == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) is the 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ Its shrine building is no longer extant and it is co-located with Mifune Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：14ページ The deities enshrined are Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (寒川比古命) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (寒川比女命). They are regarded as the guardian deities of the Tokidagawa and are the children of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命). The name “Samukawa” is an alternate name for the Tokidagawa, derived from the fact that its water was cold, which led {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|12=draft}} to name it so. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, Mifune Shrine was established by the time the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'' was compiled, that is, before the 5th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長 (元号)|lt=Enchō|12=draft}} ({{ill|927|ja|927|12=draft}}).伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ The ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'' records it as “Wahime Naishinnō Teishuku” (倭姫内親王定祝). Since it is mentioned in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'', it is inferred that the shrine existed prior to the 23rd year of {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft}} ({{ill|804|ja|804|12=draft}}). In ancient times, Toba belonged to the Tanabe area of {{ill|Togai District|ja|度会郡|12=draft}} (田辺郷). However, in the 5th year of {{ill|Taika|ja|大化|12=draft}} ({{ill|649|ja|649|12=draft}}), {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡|12=draft}} was separated from Togai District, and Toba came to belong to the Yūri area (有尓郷) of the new district. Accordingly, in the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is listed under Togai District as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine”. From the {{ill|Medieval|ja|中世|12=draft}} period onwards, the rites were discontinued, and the shrine was revived at its present location in the 3rd year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft}} ([[8月29日 (旧暦)|8月29日]] {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}}: {{ill|1663|ja|1663|12=draft}}, {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日|12=draft}}). At that time, traces of a moat ({{ill|Moat|ja|堀|12=draft}}), old pillar holes, and decayed wood were discovered, suggesting the site of the original shrine.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378 - 379ページ While {{ill|Mikoto Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|12=draft}} supported the theory that this location was the original shrine site, Nakagawa Tsuneaki argued that the village deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft}}), colloquially known as “Mifune-sha”, of a neighboring village in Toba Village, was the original shrine. In the 14th year of {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} ({{ill|1843|ja|1843|12=draft}}), an eternal lantern was installed at the shrine entrance. In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4|12=draft}} ({{ill|1871|ja|1871|12=draft}}), Mumino Shrine, whose original location had been unknown since the Medieval period, was relocated to be co‑located with Mifune Shrine. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} {{ill|Tokido Station|ja|外城田駅|12=draft}}, it is about a 17‑minute walk (approximately 900m).伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：63ページ From the station's main (southern) exit, walk along {{ill|Miyakawa Waterway|ja|宮川用水|12=draft}}, and the elevated area visible on the right is where Mifune Shrine and Mumino Shrine are located. ; By Private Car * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Tamashiro Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|lt=Tamashiro IC|lt_ja=玉城IC|12=draft}}, it is about a 14‑minute drive (approximately 5.9 {{ill|kilometer|ja|キロメートル|lt=km|12=draft}}). There is no {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|12=draft}} nearby. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|12=draft}} 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=draft}}『伊勢神宮に行こう』 Gakken Mook 神社紀行セレクション vol.1、{{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔|12=draft}} 監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』 三重県郷土資料叢書 第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』 {{ill|Kogakkan University|ja|國學院大學|12=draft}} 出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai-sha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt_ja=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_06.jpg 御船神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0701.html 御船神社（みふねじんじゃ） 御同座 牟弥乃神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11 Deprecated Open]] [[Category:Mifune Shrine (Taki Town)]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Ise]] [[Category:Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines]] [[Category:History of Taki Town]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Refreshing Imported Pages]] [[Category:Category Refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Remove New Pages From These]] [[Category:cgoodbye $1 Tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:御船神社]] [[sn:Mifune Shrine]]

Both shrines enshrine deities associated with rivers and water (*{{ill|water deities|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft|12=draft}}*).Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 64. == Overview == This section describes the common aspects of the two shrines. {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町|12=draft|12=draft}} '''Toba''' (土羽) is enshrined at the location.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378ページ The place name {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=draft|12=draft}} “Toba” (土羽) means “stopping place”, and it is derived from the boat of {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} having stopped there.金子（1983）：55ページ The shrine building {{ill|Shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}} is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} style and is surrounded by a {{ill|Gyokugaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}}. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭|12=draft|12=draft}}. An {{ill|eternal lantern|ja|常夜灯|12=draft|12=draft}} stands at the entrance of the shrine grounds. The area of the shrine grounds is 975 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|12=draft|12=draft}} (≈3,223 {{ill|square meter|ja|平方メートル|lt=m2|12=draft|12=draft}}).式内社研究会 編（1990）：380ページ == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) is the 24th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ It is the shrine established when {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, while journeying upstream on the Tokidagawa (外城田川, Tokidagawa), anchored her boat and designated the site. She stopped here because the river narrowed and her boat could no longer pass. In the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is recorded as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine” (大神乃御船神社). The term “Ōkami no Mifune” is understood to mean the boat on which {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}} rides, and Sonoda Moriyoshi conjectured that the boat-keeper’s office, which enshrined the sacred object of Amaterasu, was transferred to Mifune Shrine after her relocation. The deity enshrined is Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (大神御蔭川神).学研パブリッシング（2013）：68ページ It is regarded as either the guardian deity ({{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the Tokidagawa, which irrigates the fields, or as the protector of water transportation. The same deity is also enshrined at {{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, as a {{ill|deity|ja|神|12=draft|12=draft}}.金子（1983）：59ページ The term “Mikage” denotes a spirit or beneficence, and the ''Jingū Tenryaku'' explains that it is the spirit of the great deity enshrined by the river that diverts water to {{ill|Kanda|ja|神田|12=draft|12=draft}}.式内社研究会 編（1990）：379ページ There are alternative theories regarding the deity; the ''Ise Nisho Taijingū Shinmyōhisho'' calls it “Ōkami no Mifune Shin”, the ''Engishiki Shinmyōchō Hetsuan-shū'' refers to it as “Tenchōsen Shin”, and the ''Ise-koku Shinmyōchō Kōshō'' names it “Toriishi Kusen Shin”. == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) is the 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ Its shrine building is no longer extant and it is co-located with Mifune Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：14ページ The deities enshrined are Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (寒川比古命) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (寒川比女命). They are regarded as the guardian deities of the Tokidagawa and are the children of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命). The name “Samukawa” is an alternate name for the Tokidagawa, derived from the fact that its water was cold, which led {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} to name it so. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, Mifune Shrine was established by the time the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'' was compiled, that is, before the 5th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長 (元号)|lt=Enchō|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|927|ja|927|12=draft|12=draft}}).伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ The ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'' records it as “Wahime Naishinnō Teishuku” (倭姫内親王定祝). Since it is mentioned in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'', it is inferred that the shrine existed prior to the 23rd year of {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|804|ja|804|12=draft|12=draft}}). In ancient times, Toba belonged to the Tanabe area of {{ill|Togai District|ja|度会郡|12=draft|12=draft}} (田辺郷). However, in the 5th year of {{ill|Taika|ja|大化|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|649|ja|649|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡|12=draft|12=draft}} was separated from Togai District, and Toba came to belong to the Yūri area (有尓郷) of the new district. Accordingly, in the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is listed under Togai District as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine”. From the {{ill|Medieval|ja|中世|12=draft|12=draft}} period onwards, the rites were discontinued, and the shrine was revived at its present location in the 3rd year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft|12=draft}} ([[8月29日 (旧暦)|8月29日]] {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|1663|ja|1663|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日|12=draft|12=draft}}). At that time, traces of a moat ({{ill|Moat|ja|堀|12=draft|12=draft}}), old pillar holes, and decayed wood were discovered, suggesting the site of the original shrine.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378 - 379ページ While {{ill|Mikoto Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|12=draft|12=draft}} supported the theory that this location was the original shrine site, Nakagawa Tsuneaki argued that the village deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft|12=draft}}), colloquially known as “Mifune-sha”, of a neighboring village in Toba Village, was the original shrine. In the 14th year of {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1843|ja|1843|12=draft|12=draft}}), an eternal lantern was installed at the shrine entrance. In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1871|ja|1871|12=draft|12=draft}}), Mumino Shrine, whose original location had been unknown since the Medieval period, was relocated to be co‑located with Mifune Shrine. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Tokido Station|ja|外城田駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is about a 17‑minute walk (approximately 900m).伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：63ページ From the station's main (southern) exit, walk along {{ill|Miyakawa Waterway|ja|宮川用水|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the elevated area visible on the right is where Mifune Shrine and Mumino Shrine are located. ; By Private Car * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Tamashiro Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|lt=Tamashiro IC|lt_ja=玉城IC|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is about a 14‑minute drive (approximately 5.9 {{ill|kilometer|ja|キロメートル|lt=km|12=draft|12=draft}}). There is no {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|12=draft|12=draft}} nearby. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|12=draft|12=draft}} 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=draft|12=draft}}『伊勢神宮に行こう』 Gakken Mook 神社紀行セレクション vol.1、{{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔|12=draft|12=draft}} 監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』 三重県郷土資料叢書 第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』 {{ill|Kogakkan University|ja|國學院大學|12=draft|12=draft}} 出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai-sha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt_ja=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft|12=draft}} | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_06.jpg 御船神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0701.html 御船神社（みふねじんじゃ） 御同座 牟弥乃神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:11 Deprecated Open]] [[Category:Mifune Shrine (Taki Town)]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Ise]] [[Category:Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines]] [[Category:History of Taki Town]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Refreshing Imported Pages]] [[Category:Category Refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Remove New Pages From These]] [[Category:cgoodbye $1 Tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:御船神社]] [[sn:Mifune Shrine]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft|12=draft}} | image = [[File:Mifune-jinja-Mumino-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = Minamide 505, {{ill|Toba|ja|鳥羽|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|43.2|N|136|35|20.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Mifune Shrine: Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (*大神御蔭川神*)
Mumino Shrine: Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (*寒川比古命*) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (*寒川比女命*) | type = Mifune Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*), small-scale {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}
Mumino Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} 23 ({{ill|804 CE|ja|804|12=draft|12=draft}}) | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} (*神明造*) | festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (*神嘗祭*) }} '''{{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (*Mifune Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}), located in {{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. This article also covers the co-located '''{{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (*Mumino Jinja*), a subordinate shrine (*massha*). Both shrines enshrine deities associated with rivers and water (*{{ill|water deities|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft|12=draft}}*).Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 64. == Overview == This section describes the common aspects of the two shrines. {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町|12=draft|12=draft}} '''Toba''' (土羽) is enshrined at the location.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378ページ The place name {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=draft|12=draft}} “Toba” (土羽) means “stopping place”, and it is derived from the boat of {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} having stopped there.金子（1983）：55ページ The shrine building {{ill|Shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}} is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} style and is surrounded by a {{ill|Gyokugaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}}. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭|12=draft|12=draft}}. An {{ill|eternal lantern|ja|常夜灯|12=draft|12=draft}} stands at the entrance of the shrine grounds. The area of the shrine grounds is 975 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|12=draft|12=draft}} (≈3,223 {{ill|square meter|ja|平方メートル|lt=m2|12=draft|12=draft}}).式内社研究会 編（1990）：380ページ == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) is the 24th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ It is the shrine established when {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, while journeying upstream on the Tokidagawa (外城田川, Tokidagawa), anchored her boat and designated the site. She stopped here because the river narrowed and her boat could no longer pass. In the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is recorded as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine” (大神乃御船神社). The term “Ōkami no Mifune” is understood to mean the boat on which {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}} rides, and Sonoda Moriyoshi conjectured that the boat-keeper’s office, which enshrined the sacred object of Amaterasu, was transferred to Mifune Shrine after her relocation. The deity enshrined is Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (大神御蔭川神).学研パブリッシング（2013）：68ページ It is regarded as either the guardian deity ({{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the Tokidagawa, which irrigates the fields, or as the protector of water transportation. They are regarded as the guardian deities of the Tokidagawa and are the children of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命). The name “Samukawa” is an alternate name for the Tokidagawa, derived from the fact that its water was cold, which led {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} to name it so. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, Mifune Shrine was established by the time the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'' was compiled, that is, before the 5th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長 (元号)|lt=Enchō|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|927|ja|927|12=draft|12=draft}}).伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ The ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'' records it as “Wahime Naishinnō Teishuku” (倭姫内親王定祝). In ancient times, Toba belonged to the Tanabe area of {{ill|Togai District|ja|度会郡|12=draft|12=draft}} (田辺郷). However, in the 5th year of {{ill|Taika|ja|大化|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|649|ja|649|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡|12=draft|12=draft}} was separated from Togai District, and Toba came to belong to the Yūri area (有尓郷) of the new district. There is no {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|12=draft|12=draft}} nearby. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|12=draft|12=draft}} 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=draft|12=draft}}『伊勢神宮に行こう』 Gakken Mook 神社紀行セレクション vol.1、{{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔|12=draft|12=draft}} 監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』 三重県郷土資料叢書 第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』 {{ill|Kogakkan University|ja|國學院大學|12=draft|12=draft}} 出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai-sha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt_ja=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft|12=draft}} | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_06.jpg 御船神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0701.html 御船神社（みふねじんじゃ） 御同座 牟弥乃神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:11 Deprecated Open]] [[Category:Mifune Shrine (Taki Town)]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Ise]] [[Category:Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines]] [[Category:History of Taki Town]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Refreshing Imported Pages]] [[Category:Category Refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Remove New Pages From These]] [[Category:cgoodbye $1 Tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:御船神社]] [[sn:Mifune Shrine]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft|12=draft}} | image = [[File:Mifune-jinja-Mumino-jinja.jpg|270px]] | location = Minamide 505, {{ill|Toba|ja|鳥羽|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|43.2|N|136|35|20.6|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Mifune Shrine: Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (*大神御蔭川神*)
Mumino Shrine: Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (*寒川比古命*) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (*寒川比女命*) | type = Mifune Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*), small-scale {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}
Mumino Shrine: {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} subordinate shrine (*massha*) | Established = Before {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} 23 ({{ill|804 CE|ja|804|12=draft|12=draft}}) | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} (*神明造*) | festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (*神嘗祭*) }} '''{{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (*Mifune Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}), located in {{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. This article also covers the co-located '''{{ill|Mumino Shrine|ja|牟弥乃神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (*Mumino Jinja*), a subordinate shrine (*massha*). Both shrines enshrine deities associated with rivers and water (*{{ill|water deities|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft|12=draft}}*).Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 64. == Overview == This section describes the common aspects of the two shrines. {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Taki Town|ja|多気町|12=draft|12=draft}} '''Toba''' (土羽) is enshrined at the location.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378ページ The place name {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=draft|12=draft}} “Toba” (土羽) means “stopping place”, and it is derived from the boat of {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} having stopped there.金子（1983）：55ページ The shrine building {{ill|Shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}} is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} style and is surrounded by a {{ill|Gyokugaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}}. There is no {{ill|offertory box|ja|賽銭|12=draft|12=draft}}. An {{ill|eternal lantern|ja|常夜灯|12=draft|12=draft}} stands at the entrance of the shrine grounds. The area of the shrine grounds is 975 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|12=draft|12=draft}} (≈3,223 {{ill|square meter|ja|平方メートル|lt=m2|12=draft|12=draft}}).式内社研究会 編（1990）：380ページ == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) == Mifune Shrine (御船神社) is the 24th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ It is the shrine established when {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, while journeying upstream on the Tokidagawa (外城田川, Tokidagawa), anchored her boat and designated the site. She stopped here because the river narrowed and her boat could no longer pass. In the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is recorded as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine” (大神乃御船神社). The term “Ōkami no Mifune” is understood to mean the boat on which {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}} rides, and Sonoda Moriyoshi conjectured that the boat-keeper’s office, which enshrined the sacred object of Amaterasu, was transferred to Mifune Shrine after her relocation. The deity enshrined is Ōmikagegawa-no-Kami (大神御蔭川神).学研パブリッシング（2013）：68ページ It is regarded as either the guardian deity ({{ill|guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the Tokidagawa, which irrigates the fields, or as the protector of water transportation. The same deity is also enshrined at {{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine, as a {{ill|deity|ja|神|12=draft|12=draft}}.金子（1983）：59ページ The term “Mikage” denotes a spirit or beneficence, and the ''Jingū Tenryaku'' explains that it is the spirit of the great deity enshrined by the river that diverts water to {{ill|Kanda|ja|神田|12=draft|12=draft}}.式内社研究会 編（1990）：379ページ There are alternative theories regarding the deity; the ''Ise Nisho Taijingū Shinmyōhisho'' calls it “Ōkami no Mifune Shin”, the ''Engishiki Shinmyōchō Hetsuan-shū'' refers to it as “Tenchōsen Shin”, and the ''Ise-koku Shinmyōchō Kōshō'' names it “Toriishi Kusen Shin”. == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) == Mumino Shrine (牟弥乃神社) is the 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ Its shrine building is no longer extant and it is co-located with Mifune Shrine.宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：14ページ The deities enshrined are Samukawa-hiko-no-Mikoto (寒川比古命) and Samukawa-hime-no-Mikoto (寒川比女命). They are regarded as the guardian deities of the Tokidagawa and are the children of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命). The name “Samukawa” is an alternate name for the Tokidagawa, derived from the fact that its water was cold, which led {{ill|Wahime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} to name it so. == History == According to the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, Mifune Shrine was established by the time the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'' was compiled, that is, before the 5th year of {{ill|Enchō|ja|延長 (元号)|lt=Enchō|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|927|ja|927|12=draft|12=draft}}).伊勢文化舎（2008）：22ページ The ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'' records it as “Wahime Naishinnō Teishuku” (倭姫内親王定祝). Since it is mentioned in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'', it is inferred that the shrine existed prior to the 23rd year of {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|804|ja|804|12=draft|12=draft}}). In ancient times, Toba belonged to the Tanabe area of {{ill|Togai District|ja|度会郡|12=draft|12=draft}} (田辺郷). However, in the 5th year of {{ill|Taika|ja|大化|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|649|ja|649|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡|12=draft|12=draft}} was separated from Togai District, and Toba came to belong to the Yūri area (有尓郷) of the new district. Accordingly, in the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is listed under Togai District as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine”. From the {{ill|Medieval|ja|中世|12=draft|12=draft}} period onwards, the rites were discontinued, and the shrine was revived at its present location in the 3rd year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft|12=draft}} ([[8月29日 (旧暦)|8月29日]] {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|1663|ja|1663|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日|12=draft|12=draft}}). At that time, traces of a moat ({{ill|Moat|ja|堀|12=draft|12=draft}}), old pillar holes, and decayed wood were discovered, suggesting the site of the original shrine.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378 - 379ページ While {{ill|Mikoto Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|12=draft|12=draft}} supported the theory that this location was the original shrine site, Nakagawa Tsuneaki argued that the village deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft|12=draft}}), colloquially known as “Mifune-sha”, of a neighboring village in Toba Village, was the original shrine. In the 14th year of {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1843|ja|1843|12=draft|12=draft}}), an eternal lantern was installed at the shrine entrance. In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1871|ja|1871|12=draft|12=draft}}), Mumino Shrine, whose original location had been unknown since the Medieval period, was relocated to be co‑located with Mifune Shrine. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Tokido Station|ja|外城田駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is about a 17‑minute walk (approximately 900m).伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：63ページ From the station's main (southern) exit, walk along {{ill|Miyakawa Waterway|ja|宮川用水|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the elevated area visible on the right is where Mifune Shrine and Mumino Shrine are located. ; By Private Car * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Tamashiro Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|lt=Tamashiro IC|lt_ja=玉城IC|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is about a 14‑minute drive (approximately 5.9 {{ill|kilometer|ja|キロメートル|lt=km|12=draft|12=draft}}). There is no {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|12=draft|12=draft}} nearby. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|12=draft|12=draft}} 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=draft|12=draft}}『伊勢神宮に行こう』 Gakken Mook 神社紀行セレクション vol.1、{{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔|12=draft|12=draft}} 監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』 三重県郷土資料叢書 第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』 {{ill|Kogakkan University|ja|國學院大學|12=draft|12=draft}} 出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai-sha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt_ja=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft|12=draft}} | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_06.jpg 御船神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0701.html 御船神社（みふねじんじゃ） 御同座 牟弥乃神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:11 Deprecated Open]] [[Category:Mifune Shrine (Taki Town)]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Ise]] [[Category:Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines]] [[Category:History of Taki Town]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Refreshing Imported Pages]] [[Category:Category Refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Remove New Pages From These]] [[Category:cgoodbye $1 Tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:御船神社]] [[sn:Mifune Shrine]]

Since it is mentioned in the ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'', it is inferred that the shrine existed prior to the 23rd year of {{ill|Enryaku|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|804|ja|804|12=draft|12=draft}}). In ancient times, Toba belonged to the Tanabe area of {{ill|Togai District|ja|度会郡|12=draft|12=draft}} (田辺郷). However, in the 5th year of {{ill|Taika|ja|大化|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|649|ja|649|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Taki District|ja|多気郡|12=draft|12=draft}} was separated from Togai District, and Toba came to belong to the Yūri area (有尓郷) of the new district. Accordingly, in the ''Engishiki Shinnamyochō'', it is listed under Togai District as “Ōkami no Mifune Shrine”. From the {{ill|Medieval|ja|中世|12=draft|12=draft}} period onwards, the rites were discontinued, and the shrine was revived at its present location in the 3rd year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft|12=draft}} ([[8月29日 (旧暦)|8月29日]] {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}}: {{ill|1663|ja|1663|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|September 30|ja|9月30日|12=draft|12=draft}}). At that time, traces of a moat ({{ill|Moat|ja|堀|12=draft|12=draft}}), old pillar holes, and decayed wood were discovered, suggesting the site of the original shrine.式内社研究会 編（1990）：378 - 379ページ While {{ill|Mikoto Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|12=draft|12=draft}} supported the theory that this location was the original shrine site, Nakagawa Tsuneaki argued that the village deity ({{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft|12=draft}}), colloquially known as “Mifune-sha”, of a neighboring village in Toba Village, was the original shrine. In the 14th year of {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1843|ja|1843|12=draft|12=draft}}), an eternal lantern was installed at the shrine entrance. In {{ill|Meiji 4|ja|明治4|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1871|ja|1871|12=draft|12=draft}}), Mumino Shrine, whose original location had been unknown since the Medieval period, was relocated to be co‑located with Mifune Shrine. == Transportation == ; Public Transportation * From {{ill|JR|ja|東海旅客鉄道|lt=JR|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Tokido Station|ja|外城田駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is about a 17‑minute walk (approximately 900m).伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：63ページ From the station's main (southern) exit, walk along {{ill|Miyakawa Waterway|ja|宮川用水|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the elevated area visible on the right is where Mifune Shrine and Mumino Shrine are located. ; By Private Car * From {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Tamashiro Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|lt=Tamashiro IC|lt_ja=玉城IC|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is about a 14‑minute drive (approximately 5.9 {{ill|kilometer|ja|キロメートル|lt=km|12=draft|12=draft}}). There is no {{ill|parking lot|ja|駐車場|12=draft|12=draft}} nearby. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|12=draft|12=draft}} 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=draft|12=draft}}『伊勢神宮に行こう』 Gakken Mook 神社紀行セレクション vol.1、{{ill|Sonoda Minoru|ja|薗田稔|12=draft|12=draft}} 監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』 三重県郷土資料叢書 第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』 {{ill|Kogakkan University|ja|國學院大學|12=draft|12=draft}} 出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai-sha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt_ja=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft|12=draft}} | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_06.jpg 御船神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0701.html 御船神社（みふねじんじゃ） 御同座 牟弥乃神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:11 Deprecated Open]] [[Category:Mifune Shrine (Taki Town)]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Ise]] [[Category:Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines]] [[Category:History of Taki Town]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Taki Town]] [[Category:Refreshing Imported Pages]] [[Category:Category Refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Remove New Pages From These]] [[Category:cgoodbye $1 Tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mifune Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:御船神社]] [[sn:Mifune Shrine]]
